Provide a summary of what your application will do:
Wintip is a decentralized tipping and red pocket platform on Canton Network.  Users can send CC tips to any registered user and create red pockets  (digital gift envelopes) that multiple recipients can claim. All transactions  flow through a platform wallet using a two-step transfer pattern — sender  transfers to platform, platform transfers net amount to receiver — with a  5% platform fee automatically collected. Features include user registration  with automatic Canton party ID creation, real-time balance via Canton  Validator API, tip history, profile pages with shareable tip links  (/tip/username), and faucet distribution for new users. User testing: user: wintip-test-1 AND wintip-test-2 password: Password123!
Describe the expected users of your application:
- Content creators, streamers, and influencers who want to receive CC tips from their audience - Community members who want to reward contributors with CC - Event organizers distributing red pockets to participants - Canton Network users exploring CC utility in social applications - New users onboarding to Canton Network via the built-in faucet
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
Wintip generates Canton Coin transactions on every tip sent and every  red pocket claim. Each transaction is a two-step CC transfer: 1. Sender → Platform wallet (wintip-app) 2. Platform → Receiver  Every tip and red pocket claim generates two on-chain CC transfers,  creating Activity Marker-eligible events on the Canton ledger.  The platform intends to use Featured App Activity Markers to earn  rewards proportional to the transaction volume generated by user activity.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
Wintip interacts with the Canton ledger through the Canton Validator REST  API (Splice.Wallet.TransferOffer DAML contracts). The backend (Express.js +  TypeS

They wanted to share an update. Please see below
toggle quoted message
Show quoted text
I wanted to share a brief update following our previous note, as Wintip has continued to make strong progress on Canton testnet.
Over the past week, we have seen sustained activity from genuine users, even after a large scale bot cleanup and access restrictions. This has been especially encouraging for us, as it validates that the engagement is being driven by real community demand and meaningful product usage.
From a technical standpoint, Winsnip is now fully prepared for mainnet launch. Our validator infrastructure has already been successfully deployed across both testnet and mainnet under the Winsnip validator, and our current testnet usage metrics are publicly visible through the explorer links below.
At this stage, the remaining step is committee approval for the dApp launch on mainnet.
For reference, here are our public explorer pages:

Provide a summary of what your application will do:
Centrifuge Digital** is a Canton Network-integrated quest & engagement platform where users complete quests (social, educational, on-chain), earn points and Canton Coin rewards, submit feature/app proposals via DAML contracts, and interact with the Canton Network through a built-in browser wallet — all hosted at centrifuge.digital and canton.centrifuge.digital.
Describe the expected users of your application:
Crypto-native users from the POSTHUMAN validator community, Canton Network enthusiasts, and DeFi participants who want to learn about and engage with Canton Network through gamified quests, wallet interaction, and governance proposals.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
Every user action (quest completion, wallet connect, proposal submission, voting, referrals, daily logins) triggers the Go backend to exercise `CreateActivityMarker` on the `FeaturedAppRight` contract on the Canton ledger. More ActivityMarkers generated → more Canton Coin (CC) distributed to the POSTHUMAN validator in DSO reward rounds. CC is then partially retained by the operator and partially distributed to users via a reward pool.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
The Go backend communicates with the Canton participant node via gRPC (Ledger API). It creates DAML contracts (`ActivityLog`, `QuestCompletion`, `AppProposal`, `WalletRegistration`) and, post-Splice onboarding, exercises `FeaturedAppRight.CreateActivityMarker` on the Splice/Amulet package. The wallet allocates Canton parties on-ledger. Contract verification uses `GetUpdateById` gRPC calls.
Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.
Yes, Activity Markers. Reward-earning activities: Wallet connect - 1 - Once per user; Quest completion - 1 - Per task; Proposal submission - 3

Name of the application:

URL for the public code repository (if available)
https://saxon-xyz.github.io/canton-keeper-docs/
Provide a summary of what your application will do:
Canton Keeper is a config-driven automation daemon for Canton Network validators. It watches Daml contracts on the ledger and exercises choices based on YAML-defined triggers — deadlines, multi-leg settlement matching, contract existence, and periodic intervals. Canton's privacy model means no third party can automate transactions on your behalf — every validator needs its own keeper. CK solves this with a single daemon, configurable via YAML, requiring no Daml expertise to operate. It follows the same automation patterns used internally by Splice.
Describe the expected users of your application:
All Canton Network validators (~1000). Any validator operator wanting automated contract lifecycle management — settlement, expiry, billing, and cleanup. CK is distributed as a free public Docker image (
ghcr.io/saxon-xyz/canton-keeper
) for frictionless adoption. Validators configure automation rules in a YAML file and deploy alongside their participant node.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
CK generates real ledger transactions by exercising Daml choices on behalf of the validator's operator party. Every transaction is tagged with a FeaturedAppRight activity marker. The more jobs a validator configures, the more transactions CK generates, the more activity markers are created. CK is designed to scale across all Canton validators — each instance independently generates rewarded network activity.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
CK connects to the validator's Canton participant node via the gRPC Ledger API (v2). It authenticates using Auth0 M2M (machine-to-machine) client credentials and acts as the validator's operator party. It streams contract ev

Provide a summary of what your application will do:
CompassSwap is an automated market maker (AMM) built natively on the Canton Network. It enables permissionless token swaps between Canton Coin (CC), USDCx, and other CIP56-standard tokens using an intent-based deposit system—no manual confirmations required for each settlement step. Developers can also embed our swap functionality directly into their own dApps via the CompassSwap SDK and REST API. Categories: Liquidity, Exchange, Developer Tools.
Describe the expected users of your application:
1.Canton Network participants looking to swap between CC, USDCx, and other CIP-56 tokens without custodial risk. 2.Liquidity providers seeking to generate passive yield through fee distributions on every trade. 3.Third-party developers and institutional partners integrating native exchange capabilities into their Canton applications via our decentralized SDK.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
For activity rewards, CompassSwap employs a custom Daml contract that atomically bundles each swap's output transfer with a FeaturedAppActivityMarker creation—one marker per completed swap, ensuring zero user friction. The execution layer is token-agnostic, working with any CIP-56 TransferFactory. Markers are created exclusively for economically significant swap completions per CIP-47 guidelines.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
CompassSwap interacts with the Canton ledger through the Ledger API on our self-hosted Validator node. The backend monitors TransactionTree updates to detect incoming token deposits on swap pool addresses, then automatically executes trades by exercising contract choices (constant product x·y=k). messages.body:
Featured App Submission Details
Submitted:
2026-04-02 04:14:10
Entry ID:
745
Featured App Request:
Aurtech Group Holdings Ltd
Name of the application:
Aurpay Merchant Privacy Rail
Disclaimer
You may not use the Canton name, logo, or any other Canton trademark in, or as part of, your product or service name.
BRAND GUIDLINES
Email
rabi@...
Name of applying institution
Aurtech Group Holdings Ltd
Summary of Company and Background
Aurpay is a crypto payment gateway for ecommerce merchants. We provide non-custodial checkout plugins for Shopify, WooCommerce, and BigCommerce that let online stores accept cryptocurrency payments. We're a registered Money Services Business (MSB) in HongKong. Our merchants currently process BTC, ETH, USDC, and USDT payments on Ethereum and other EVM chains. We've been operating since 2022 and serve merchants across retail, SaaS, and digital goods. We're integrating Canton because our merchants need something Ethereum can't give them: transaction privacy. On Ethereum, every settlement is public — competitors can see volumes, pricing, and supplier relationships on Etherscan. On Canton, only the parties involved see the transaction. That's why we're building Canton settlement into Aurpay — to give merchants private, confidential payments with CC and USDCx, while filling a gap we identified in the Canton ecosystem: there is no merchant payment gateway among the 300+ Canton partners today.
Name of the application:
Aurpay Merchant Privacy Rail
URL of the applying institution
https://aurpay.net
Product Website
https://aurpay.net
Emails for Responsible Persons
rabi@...
Party ID for the Featured Application
aurpay-validator-1::122027d4cc22b12ccc9cfcf620692d630bd2eddd34ce93fe40697d71f6e1fbfa30c3
Provide a summary of what your application will do:
Aurpay Canton Settlement lets ecommerce merchants move their payment revenue onto Canton Network. Merchants accept crypto payments through Aurpay's existing checkout plugins (Shopify, WooCommerce, BigCommerce) — shoppers pay in any cryptocurrency on Ethereum, same as before, no Canton involvement on the buyer side. After Aurpay collects the payment, the merchant decides what to do with the funds: keep them in Aurpay, bridge them to Canton via xReserve as USDCx, or split between both. Once funds are on Canton as USDCx, merchants can hold them privately or send USDCx to other parties on Canton — suppliers, partners, other businesses — with full transaction privacy. Only the parties involved see the transfer. Each bridged settlement creates an activity marker. There is currently no merchant payment gateway in the Canton ecosystem — Aurpay fills that gap.
Describe the expected users of your application:
Our users are online merchants — ecommerce stores on Shopify, WooCommerce, and BigCommerce, marketplace operators, and B2B businesses. They already accept crypto through Aurpay and we are giving them the option to move their funds onto Canton as USDCx for private settlement. Merchants can also se

Summary of Company and Background
Avro Digital is a Canton Network infrastructure company building market products for institutional and retail participants. Avro Exchange is a batch auction exchange for Canton asset pairs.
Name of the application:
Avro Exchange
URL of the applying institution
https://www.avrofi.com/
Product Website
https://www.avrofi.com/
Emails for Responsible Persons
ian@...
Party ID for the Featured Application
avro-exchange::12209bdf08fc008d18cca74c36dbc7930ca196a7c67d28f44d836f066f4a613cd84d
URL for the public code repository (if available)
Private repository; available to the Committee upon request.
Link to Brand Materials (optional)
Available upon request.
Provide a summary of what your application will do:
Avro Exchange is a **Dual Flow Batch Auction (DFBA)** exchange for Canton Network assets. Traders submit buy and sell orders during a batch window, then all orders in the batch are matched at a single **uniform clearing price** to eliminating front-running.  Core functionality: - **Submit orders** — buy or sell Canton assets with limit prices and minimum fill amounts - **Batch matching** — orders are grouped into time-windowed batches and matched via a Coincidence of Wants (CoW) algorithm - **On-chain settlement** — matched trades settle through Canton ledger contracts (FundLock → MatchRecord → Settlement), providing cryptographic proof of every trade - **Automatic rollover** — unmatched orders roll to the next batch
Describe the expected users of your application:
- Institutional Canton participants (market makers, treasuries, validators) seeking fair-price execution on Canton asset pairs - Individual CC holders looking to trade - Liquidity providers and market makers providing depth across pairs - Other Canton applications integrating via API for programmatic trading
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
**Canton Coin interaction:** - CC is a directly tradeable asset on the exchange - Users deposit CC to place orders; CC transfers use the standard Splice Amulet transfer mechanism - The exchange operator's party is set as the **provider** on all CC transfers, earning provider-field reward weight automatically  **Activity Markets:** - Each batch match creates on-chain Canton contracts (FundLock, MatchRecord, Settlement) — genuine economic activity with full audit trail
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
All exchange activity creates Canton ledger contracts: Order submission, batch matching, settlement, cancellations The exchange uses the Canton JSON

Provide a summary of what your application will do:
This application creates and manages ClearLoop Tokens on Canton so that ClearLoop balances and non-public asset movements can be represented on-chain in a privacy-preserving and auditable way.  It acts as the token issuer and token lifecycle layer for ClearLoop, allowing clients and trading venues to gain verifiable visibility into ClearLoop activity without changing the existing ClearLoop operating model.
Describe the expected users of your application:
Existing and prospective ClearLoop clients, including trading firms, hedge funds, and asset managers, as well as connected trading venues that require an auditable token representation of ClearLoop balances and asset movements.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
The application will interact with the Canton Network by minting, burning, and transferring ClearLoop Tokens in response to ClearLoop balance events that need to be represented through the token layer.  The main use cases are deposit minting, withdrawal burning, and the token transfers required to maintain the ClearLoop token lifecycle on-chain. Settlement uses the same token model, but the operational submission of settlement-related Canton transactions is handled by the separate ClearLoop Settlement application.  For the ClearLoop Tokens Featured Application, Activity Markers would be created via Digital Asset's Utilities as part of these token lifecycle actions.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
The application will use the ledger to establish and maintain the token representation of ClearLoop balances and movements.  Deposits result in minting to the relevant Canton party. Delegations and undelegations result in transfers between a client's main

How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
The application will interact with the Canton Network by submitting the transactions required to reflect ClearLoop settlement outcomes on-chain.  Its primary use case is the operational execution of settlement-related Canton transactions between the relevant ClearLoop parties once ClearLoop has determined that a settlement must occur. Unlike ClearLoop Tokens, this application is not responsible for establishing the token model itself or for the Digital Asset Utilities-based token lifecycle flows. Its role is to execute the settlement workflow that uses the existing token model created by ClearLoop Tokens.  The application would create its own Activity Markers for the operational settlement transactions it performs on Canton in order to help cover the cost of settlement activity on-chain.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
The application will use the ledger to submit the transactions that reflect realised ClearLoop settlement outcomes between the relevant parties.  When ClearLoop deter

Provide a summary of what your application will do:
Clareos is a settlement oracle for prediction markets on the Canton Network. Unlike existing oracles that focus on asset pricing for DeFi and tokenization, Clareos is purpose-built for prediction market resolution — publishing real-world outcome data and issuing immutable on-ledger settlement attestations as auditable proof of market resolution. It covers 70+ data feeds across 10 categories — crypto, equities, rates, commodities, FX, macroeconomics, Fed policy, sports, esports, and earnings.
Describe the expected users of your application:
Prediction market operators on the Canton Network (e.g., Nexode, Unhedged). Operators run markets — such as FOMC rate decision outcomes, crypto price thresholds, CPI releases, or sports results — that reference Clareos data feeds. When a market's resolution window closes, the operator submits a SettlementRequest. Clareos verifies the outcome against its published data and issues an immutable SettlementAttestation — the on-ledger proof that the market was resolved correctly.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
Every datapoint Clareos publishes and every settlement it performs is recorded on-ledger as a Daml contract. When a prediction market is settled — the moment of genuine economic resolution — Clareos creates an activity marker through the Splice FeaturedAppRightV1 API. Markers are only created at settlement, never during routine data publishing, so rewards are tied directly to real economic activity.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
Clareos publishes data feeds to the ledger continuously for financial assets like crypto and equities,

Thank you for the feedback on the initial submission. Since then, we've overhauled the architecture to be purely demand-driven. Operators browse available events through an off-ledger catalog and enter into on-chain subscription agreements specifying which event categories they want to receive. If no operators have subscribed there is zero on-ledger activity.

Provide a summary of what your application will do:
Apex is a skill-based sequence competition running on the Canton Network. Each round, participants purchase tickets by submitting 7-digit sequences of their choosing. At round close, the winner is determined by the maximum Hamming distance isolation score. The participant whose sequence is most distinct from all other submissions wins. Because victory depends on choosing a sequence that is maximally different from the field, Apex rewards strategic reasoning and information asymmetry rather than luck. All ticket submissions, winner scoring, and prize distribution are executed via DAML smart contracts on Canton MainNet, providing sealed submissions, verifiable scoring, and winner-controlled prize claims. The prize pool is 95% of ticket revenue; 5% is retained by the platform.
Describe the expected users of your application:
Apex is designed for three user archetypes:  1. Strategy and puzzle enthusiasts: individuals who enjoy game-theoretic competition, coordination problems, and information-asymmetry games. The Hamming distance scoring mechanism rewards players who reason about what the field will submit and differentiate accordingly. No specialist financial knowledge is required.  2. Provide a summary of what your application will do:
P100 is a constraint-aware prediction market platform where users trade binary outcome contracts using a Frequent Batch Auction (FBA) clearing mechanism. Unlike continuous-order-book venues, P100 accumulates orders over a configurable window and clears them simultaneously at a single uniform price. This eliminates front-running, MEV, and latency arbitrage by design. All order submission, batch clearing, position issuance, and market resolution execute entirely on-chain via DAML smart contracts on the Canton Network. Users post capital-efficient collateral (a buyer posting probability p posts p per unit; a seller posts 1 minus p), receive transferable position tokens upon fill, and settle atomically at resolution. The platform charges 25 basis points per matched unit, split proportionally between buyer and seller based on their absolute potential upside.
Describe the expected users of your application:
P100 is designed for four primary user archetypes: (1) Retail prediction market participants: individuals who form views on discrete binary events (financial benchmarks, macroeconomic outcomes, asset price levels) and wish to express those views with defined-risk positions. Users need no prior options or derivatives experience; collateral require

Describe the expected users of your application:
Any user who wants to manage their capital on Canton can save time and energy using Blueprint.  They can use existing capital on any major network to deploy it to trade or earn - Blueprint automates any necessary swaps and transactions.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?

Users will earn application rewards for deposits.

We'll have activity markers every time users deposit and our agents rebalance portfolios.
On a per user basis, what is your expected daily number of transactions
~50-100 transactions
Under what conditions may a user generate multiple transactions per round/epoch?
Because a user will have a corresponding autonomous agent, the agent will continuously execute transactions on their behalf.

Linear
What is your anticipated launch date on MainNet?
Early April
Who will be your first customers and what is the expected go-live dates?
Our first customers will be existing users who will already be able to use Blueprint to interact on Canton as well as existing Canton users who want an easier way to interact with the network.
How would not having FA status change your operating plans?
It would minimize how much we can incentivize users on other chains to transition to Canton.  We can still launch on Canton, but would expect slower growth as other networks would be prioritized.

Provide a summary of what your application will do:
Cardiv is an institutional-grade Limit Order Book exchange built on the Canton Network. It enables professional participants to trade CIP-0056 tokenized assets with fully on-chain, non-custodial settlement. The platform provides a transparent order book, deterministic Delivery-versus-Payment (DvP) atomic settlement, and non-custodial asset management throughout the full trading lifecycle. Users place limit and market orders directly from their Canton wallets. Assets are locked on-chain to guarantee settlement, matched orders settle atomically within a single Canton transaction tree, and unmatched orders are cancelled with immediate on-chain asset release.
Describe the expected users of your application:
Cardiv targets institutional and professional participants: digital asset traders, market makers, proprietary firms, asset managers, and crypto desks. These users require precise limit orders, deterministic settlement, and non‑custodial control,  features AMM‑based exchanges cannot provide.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
Cardiv integrates with the Canton ledger through three transaction types, each consuming Canton traffic fees and generating Activity Markers tied to meaningful trading events: Order Placement & Token Lock – The Wallet Provider submits a transaction that creates an order contract, performs a CIP‑0056 self‑transfer, and locks assets with Cardiv as lock holder. This reserves liquidity for settlement and adds an Activity Marker. Order Matching & Settlement – Cardiv submits a transaction that unlocks reserved holdings and executes a CIP‑0056 allocation with two transfer legs. Settlement is atomic within a single transaction tree, ensuring both sides succeed or neither does. An Activity Marker is added for the final asset transfer. Order Cancellation /

chris@...
Name of applying institution
CryptoLegacy
Summary of Company and Background
CryptoLegacy is the world's first Decentralized Liveness Oracle and digital asset inheritance network built on Canton and Daml. Recognizing the growing challenge of secure digital wealth succession, our mission is to ensure digital wealth is safely passed on in a completely trustless, P2P manner. We leverage Canton's sub-transaction privacy to decouple "liveness verification" from "asset management." This enables a decentralized network of "Guardians" to earn bounties by verifying users' survival status, while strictly guaranteeing that asset details remain completely invisible to Verifiers and the platform itself.
Name of the application:
CryptoLegacy
URL of the applying institution
https://www.cryptolegacy.online
Product Website
https://www.cryptolegacy.online
Party ID for the Featured Application
cryptolegacy::122002cb5bad0f12132febb896a511d40b7d542e0b9cde83b415b5c7148a9d2419c3
URL for the public code repository (if available)
https://github.com/cryptolegacyHQ/daml-impl
Provide a summary of what your application will do:
CryptoLegacy acts as a Decentralized Guardian Network where users can publish "Liveness Bounties." Independent "Guardians" stake tokens to accept these verification tasks, earning Canton Coin by periodically interacting with users to verify their living status. If a user fails to respond after a grace period, their encrypted inheritance plans are automatically triggered for their designated heirs.
Describe the expected users of your application:
The expected users of our application are individuals who hold digital assets (such as cryptocurrencies and NFTs) and are looking for a secure, privacy-preserving way to manage their inheritance. They are typically privacy-conscious users who want to guarantee their wealth is seamlessly passed to their chosen heirs without exposing their asset details to third-party platforms during their lifetime.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
Our application interacts with the Canton Network exclusively through highly valuable, P2P economic transactions driven by our Decentralized Guardian Network:  1.Bounty Funding and Staking: Users lock Canton Coin into P2P Bounty Pools to pay for long-term inheritance monitoring. Similarly, Guardians must stake Canton Coin to accept verification jobs, creating significant Total Value Locked (TVL) and staking utility. 2.Ping-Pong Liveness Market (Service Exchange): Guardians initiate "Ping" requests and users respond with "Pong" signatures. Upon successful verification, Smart Contracts

Provide a summary of what your application will do:
Canton Prediction Markets is a curated weekly prediction market running entirely on Canton. Each week, the community votes on a single binary question from 3–5 candidates, stakes into Yes/No via a simple parimutuel pool, and a designated resolver settles the outcome on-ledger using DAML templates and USDC.x. The goal is to demonstrate visible ledger liveness through a ritualized cadence — vote → stake → resolve → recap — giving Canton stakeholders a clear, recurring heartbeat of real on-chain activity.
Describe the expected users of your application:
Initial users are Canton ecosystem participants, community members, and Canton Network stakeholders who want to engage with prediction markets as a demonstration of ledger liveness. Senior Canton leadership and ecosystem developers are also expected to follow the weekly market cycle as a visible proof-of-activity signal.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
The application runs a single binary parimutuel pool per week entirely on Canton using DAML smart contracts. Each pool lifecycle (open → trading → resolve → settle) generates on-ledger transactions settled in USDC.x. Every stake placement, pool state update, and resolution event is a Canton transaction, creating a consistent and visible stream of ledger activity. This recurring weekly cadence directly contributes to Canton Network activity metrics and can be leveraged for application rewards tied to transaction volume and ledger engagement.

The application uses a single DAML binary pool template deployed on Canton. Key ledger interactions include:  - **Pool creation:*

messages.body:

Disclaimer

BRAND GUIDLINES
Email
mehmet@...
Name of applying institution
LynoBridge
Summary of Company and Background
LynoBridge is a cross-chain bridge protocol that enables users to convert any token on any EVM-compatible L2 network into USDCx on the Canton Network. The process operates in two layers: token swaps are handled through Relay Link infrastructure, after which USDC is transferred to Canton via Circle’s CCTP protocol and minted as USDCx. Users do not need to manage these two layers separately; LynoBridge combines both steps into a single seamless flow. This architecture opens the Canton ecosystem to Ethereum L2 liquidity while giving users full flexibility in token selection.
Name of the application:
LynoBridge
URL of the applying institution
https://lynobridge.com
Product Website
https://lynobridge.com
Emails for Responsible Persons
mehmet@...
bohdan@...
nida@...
Party ID for the Featured Application
lynobridge-validator-1::1220763122479f93377a02299cb6ab573f96b389f944474ecd475644ce232aeef3db
Provide a summary of what your application will do:
LynoBridge is the easiest way to bring Ethereum L2 liquidity to the Canton Network. Regardless of which token a user holds, they can receive USDCx on Canton in just a few steps: select a token, enter an amount, confirm. Relay Link handles the swap and CCTP handles the Canton transfer in the background; users never interact with this complexity directly. Thanks to the embedded wallet infrastructure, users do not need to set up a separate Canton wallet. The platform combines wallet creation and bridging into a single flow, allowing even non-technical users to complete the process within minutes. USDCx on Canton can be used directly in other ecosystem applications, settlement operations, and DvP mechanisms. LynoBridge makes Canton accessible to everyday users.
Describe the expected users of your application:
LynoBridge’s primary target audience is individual retail users who hold liquidity on Ethereum L2 networks. The platform is designed for users who may not be familiar with the Canton ecosystem or its technical infrastructure but wish to move their assets to Canton. Over 12,000 pre-registrations and 850+ transactions during closed DevNet testing demonstrate concrete demand. In later stages, institutional users seeking to leverage Canton’s privacy-preserving infrastructure for tokenized asset settlement and inter-institutional transfers will also be onboarded.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
Upon completion of each CCTP cycle, USDCx is minted or burned on Canton. Each transaction is stamped with an Activity Marker and included in application

Name of applying institution
Avro Digital
Summary of Company and Background
Avro Digital is a Canton Network participant building market infrastructure and applications that generate economically meaningful, monitorable ledger activity.  This Featured App request is for xCC Vault, a Canton Coin (CC) pool product. Users deposit CC into a vault and receive **xCC**, a transferable pool-share token representing a proportional claim on pooled CC. Rewards accruing to the pool increase the **CC-per-xCC exchange rate** over time, so xCC holders accrue value pro rata without per-user reward distributions.  xCC Vault is the first product in the Avro market stack. Over time, we plan to launch additional applications (submitted separately for review/approval where applicable) that may route a disclosed portion of their own rewards to xCC Vault for the benefit of xCC depositors (subject to tokenomics committee governance).

xCC Vault

Provide a summary of what your application will do:
xCC Vault lets users deposit **Canton Coin (CC)** into a vault and receive **xCC**, a pool-share token. The vault maintains on-ledger accounting for total CC held and total xCC issued; the **exchange rate** (CC per xCC) increases as rewards accrue into the pool.  Users can:  * **Deposit CC → receive xCC** * **Transfer xCC** (CIP-56-compatible token behavior) * **Redeem xCC via request \+ claim:** submit a redemption request, wait a redemption period, then claim CC by burning xCC
Describe the expected users of your application:
* Institutional Canton participants (validators, market makers, treasuries) holding CC who want a simple pool-share product * Individual CC holders * Protocols and integrators that want a CC-denominated pool-share token that follows Canton token standards (CIP-56)
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
The application uses CC as the underlying asset and implements pool-share accounting on-ledger.  Core flows:  1. **Deposit:** user transfers CC into the pool and receives newly minted xCC at the prevailing exchange rate.  2. **Accrual:** rewards received by the pool increase total CC without increasing total xCC, raising CC-per-xCC for all holders.  3. **Transfer:** xCC can be transferred between parties a

An initial conservative estimate for planning is ~3–5 transactions per user per day on average across the user base.

ClearEcho is a native privacy-preserving oracle system designed specifically for the Canton Network, dedicated to serving RWA (Real-World Assets), DeFi, and institutional-grade financial scenarios. The core problem we solve is the exposure of commercial secrets caused by traditional oracles (such as Chainlink) broadcasting data across public chains. Leveraging Canton's sub-transaction privacy features and Daml's native permission model, ClearEcho provides extremely pure, high-granularity, and tamper-proof off-chain data sources while ensuring strict directional authorization and privacy isolation of data, providing a "clear" data foundation for institutions entering Web3.

As a seamless native Daml component, it provides reliable institutional-grade infrastructure for RWA pricing anchors and DeFi liquidations on Canton.

Institutional Liquidity Providers (LPs): Funds and corporate treasuries looking for real yield generated from transaction fees.  Canton-based dApps & DeFi Protocols: Financial applications requiring deep, instant liquidity to function effectively.  Arbitrageurs & Traders: High-frequency traders balancing asset prices across networks.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
PoolX is entirely designed as a "Tokenless Protocol". We do not have a native utility token; instead, all platform fees, cross-chain settlements, and liquidity operations are executed natively using Canton Coin (CC). This drives immense utility and transaction volume to CC. Activity Markers will be generated when liquidity providers deposit/withdraw assets and when Canton dApps route trade executions through PoolX's Daml-governed smart contracts.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
Our infrastructure uses Daml smart contracts to govern liquidity pools with strict privacy and compliance controls. When external assets are bridged, they are minted 1:1 as Daml-wrapped tokens on the Canton ledger. Trade executions, asset swaps, and yield distributions are all immutably recorded and settled on the Canton Network, ensuring deterministic execution for institutional players.
Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.
Liquidity Pr

The executor is token-agnostic, working with any CIP-56 TransferFactory. Markers are only created for economically significant swap completions per CIP-47 guidelines.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
OneSwap interacts with the Canton ledger through the Ledger API on our self-hosted validator node. The backend streams TransactionTree updates to detect incoming token deposits on swap and LP party addresses, then automatically executes swaps by exercising pool contracts (constant product x·y=k) and transferring output tokens to users via CIP-56 TransferFactory contracts — CC through the Scan API and USDCx through the Utilities API. For featured app rewards, each swap output transfer is bundled atomic

Describe the expected users of your application:
Raven Market is intended for active crypto traders, proprietary trading desks, liquidity providers, and sophisticated on-chain participants who want structured, fixed-payout exposure to asset price movements. It may also support permissioned deployments for institutional participants where required.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
Raven Market will interact with the Canton ledger by creating and managing DAML contracts that represent options series, user positions, and liquidity pools; each user action (quote/trade, mint/burn position, deposit/withdraw liquidity) is recorded as a ledger transaction that updates on-ledger state deterministically. At expiry, the application records price resolution and executes atomic settlement by transferring Canton Coin (CC) payouts from pool reserves to winning position holders, while retaining protocol fees and updating pool accounting on-ledger.
Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.
Raven Market will earn application rewards from on-ledger activity generated by (1) options market creation and parameter updates, (2) user trading activity, including opening/closing positions and related pool rebalancing, and (3) contract expiry workflows, including resolution and atomic payout settlement in Canton Coin (CC).

Without FA status we can still operate, but would reduce transaction throughput.

Provide a summary of what your application will do:
The xCC Vault lets users deposit Canton Coin (CC) into a vault and receive xCC, a pool-share token. The pool maintains on-ledger accounting for total CC held and total xCC issued; the exchange rate (CC per xCC) increases as rewards accrue into the pool. users can Deposit CC -> receive xCC, transfer xcc (cip56), Redeem xcc via request + claim.
Describe the expected users of your application:
Institutional Canton participants (validators, market makers, treasuries) holding CC who want a simple pool-share product Individual CC holders Protocols and integrators that want a CC-denominated pool-share token that follows Canton token standards (CIP-56)
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
The application uses CC as the underlying asset and implements pool-share accounting on-ledger. Core flows: Deposit: user transfers CC into the pool and receives newly minted xCC at the prevailing exchange rate. Accrual: rewards received by the pool increase total CC without increasing total xCC, raising CC-per-xCC for all holders. Transfer: xCC can be transferred between parties as a standard token. Redeem (request + claim): Request: user requests redemption; xCC is locked/pending during cooldown. Claim (after cooldown): user claims by burning xCC and receiving CC at the prevailing exchange rate. Reinforcement by future apps: Additional applications in the future (submitted independently for review), will be configured to route a disclosed portion of their rewards to the xCC pool so that xCC depositors benefit via the exchange-rate mechanism. This is a beneficiary/routing choice and does not require creating extra marker volume beyond those apps’ own economically meaningful actions.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
Primarily for settlement

messages.body:
New Featured App Submission Details
Submitted:
2026-02-18 19:32:02
Entry ID:
456
Email
mike@...
Name of applying institution
Etherfuse
Summary of Company and Background
Etherfuse is a blockchain-based platform that tokenizes real-world assets, specifically sovereign debt. The platform is designed to expand access to financial products by bridging traditional finance (TradFi) and decentralized finance (DeFi). Integrating traditional instruments with blockchain infrastructure enabling assets that look like fiat, act like a bond, and move like crypto.  Etherfuse was launched in early 2021 by co-founders and brothers, David Taylor and AJ Taylor with the goal of making traditional financial instruments more accessible and lowering the cost of moving money across borders. Later that year, Etherfuse began regulatory engagement to establish a working framework for tokenized sovereign debt with Mexican regulators, including the Ministry of Finance and the National Banking and Securities Commission (CNBV).   In October 2023, the company officially unveiled its first Stablebond, CETES (Mexican Federal Treasury Certificates) to be sold as private offerings for non-U.S. persons. By April 2024, the CNBV issued Resolution P090/2024, which provided “no action relief.” The resolution stated the Stablebonds did not require prior authorization to be sold because assets are held within government-regulated financial institutions. This milestone allowed Etherfuse to transition from a restricted public offering to being the first and only Mexican blockchain company with regulatory clarity to engage the general public.   Following this verdict, Etherfuse began to expand its product offerings to include tokenized debt from the United States, Brazil, and various European assets. In partnership with Brale, it then launched Sovereign Coins, introducing MXNe (Real MXN), a stablecoin backed by Stablebonds.   In January 2026, Etherfuse announced a strategic partnership with Shinhan Bank in South Korea to begin tokenizing sovereign debt across Asia, thus launching the most recent Stablebond, the Korean Treasury Bond (KTB).

ZapioPay also offers a developer-friendly Payment API that anyone can embed on their website to accept Canton-native payments — think “Buy Me a Coffee” but powered by Canton. Whether it’s a creator collecting tips, a freelancer invoicing a client, or a small business accepting payments, ZapioPay makes it dead simple to send and receive value on Canton.
This will be used for social media/website descriptions
4
Describe the expected users of your application:
ZapioPay is specifically designed to bring new, non-crypto-native users into Canton. Our primary users include: (1) Everyday consumers who want a fun, simple way to send and receive payments without needing to understand blockchain infrastructure, (2) Content creators, freelancers, and community builders who want to embed a Canton payment widget on their website or social profiles to receive tips and payments via ZapioPay’s API, (3) Small businesses and merchants looking for an easy way to accept Canton-native payments online, and (4) Existing Canton ecosystem participants who want a more engaging, social payments experience for everyday transfers.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
Canton Coin is a core payment asset within the app — users can send, receive, and tip CC natively, making it the fun default currency for zaps and tips. The Payment API also enables CC-denominated payment buttons on external websites, driving CC utility beyond the Canton ecosystem into the broader web. 2. Name of applying institution

3. Summary of Company and Background
Prime Grid Solutions is building verifiable randomness infrastructure for the Canton Network. We are a pre-revenue, launch-stage company. Our product — Prime, a Verifiable Random Function (VRF) service built on Ed25519 elliptic curve cryptography — is currently operational on LocalNet with no paying customers yet. We are applying for Featured App status as a launch application, with the intent to go live on MainNet and grow an independent, fee-based developer business.

1::1220ae443a638065d6444936ae1740bf864da098ee749928ed65083e76022b47d9c6

(your GitHub repo URL if public, or leave blank)
10. Link to Brand Materials
(optional — logo/brand assets link)
11. Demo Video
(optional)
12. Provide a summary of what your application will do
Prime gives Canton developers access to cryptographically verifiable randomness — a capability the network currently lacks. A developer calls Prime's REST API or native Daml library and receives a random value together with an Ed25519 cryptographic proof that anyone can independently verify. The proof is permanently recorded as an on-chain contract on the Canton ledger, creating an immutable, tamper-proof audit trail.
The service is offered through a self-service developer portal with bundle-based pricing sized to Canton's ledger fee structure (approximately $0.12 per on-chain contract write at ~2 KB per
contract at $60/MB). Each on-chain proof write is the cost basis; subscription revenue must cover those fees plus operating margin.
Pricing tiers:
Free
— $0/month, 10 requests/month (evaluation only), no markers
Starter
— $99/month, 250 requests/month (~$30 in Canton fees, ~70% gross margin)
Growth
— $299/month, 900 requests/month (~$108 in Canton fees, ~64% gross margin)
Scale
— $999/month, 3,500 requests/month (~$420 in Canton fees, ~58% gross margin)
Enterprise
— custom volume and pricing
Overage
— $0.50/request beyond bundle
Revenue comes from monthly subscription fees paid by developers — not from Canton Rewards. Activity Markers are submitted only for paid-tier (Starter, Growth, and Scale) on-chain contract creation transactions; the Free tier generates no markers.
13. Describe the expected users of your application
Canton application developers who need randomness they can prove is fair. The clearest use cases are:
DeFi protocols
requiring unbiased ordering for liquidations, distributions, or auctions
Gaming and predicti

Thank you for the helpful feedback — your point is valid. To improve accuracy and transparency, we propose distinguishing activity in two ways:
Execution activity
— when a Canton application actively consumes RedStone price data to perform on-chain business logic, such as settling trades, triggering liquidations, updating market states, processing margin requirements, or executing other contract-level actions. In this case, oracle data directly drives economic outcomes and system state transitions.
Availability guarantee
— when applications require SLA-backed assurances that on-chain data remains accurate (e.g., within 1% of market price) and fresh (e.g., updated at least every 24h). Although RedStone Oracles, as a featured app on Canton, monitor markets and on-chain state every few seconds, only the updates necessary to satisfy these SLA requirements should be marked as activity.

Activity Markers are generated in alignment with user activity, including minting, redemption, and on-ledger transfers of cETH, following standard CIP-56 issuer patterns used by DA Utilities

0–3 transactions per day for typical users, with higher activity for power users and integrated applications

active trading, batch payments, or mint and transfer sequences within applications

topic.title: New Featured App Request: Yogen

messages.body:
New Featured App Submission Details
Submitted:
2026-02-09 01:10:25
Entry ID:
392
Email
alex@...
Name of applying institution
Yogen
Summary of Company and Background
We just built something that doesn't exist yet. A prediction market on Canton Network.  Everyone knows Polymarket. It's the gold standard for prediction markets. But it runs on Ethereum, and Ethereum has problems that nobody's solved. Front-running. Public order books where bots see your trade before it clears. Zero privacy. Institutions won't touch it because of the regulatory mess.  So we took the entire concept and moved it to Canton. That's the play. Canton gives us everything Ethereum can't. Private transactions by default. No MEV, no front-running, no bots jumping your orders. It was purpose-built for institutional finance by Digital Asset, and the regulatory framework actually makes sense. We're not fighting the system. We're building on the right one.  The platform is called Yogen. It means "prophecy" in Japanese. And it's real. Not a pitch deck, not a mockup. It's a working product.  The pricing engine runs on LMSR. Same math Polymarket, Augur, and Gnosis use. Prices adjust automatically as people trade. You bet on outcomes, the market finds the price, and winners get paid. It works.  We shipped a full stack over the past two weeks. FastAPI backend with 50 Python files handling the trading logic. Next.js frontend with 13 pages, real-time price charts, and portfolio tracking. Nine Daml smart contracts for on-chain settlement. Supabase database with row-level security. Full admin dashboard for market creation, resolution, user management, and settlements.  Security wasn't an afterthought. We put the platform through seven independent audit passes covering the backend, frontend, smart contracts, and infrastructure. The final security score came out to 100 out of 100. That's not a marketing number. That's the actual result after methodically auditing every layer of the stack.  Both repos are live on GitHub. The frontend is deployed on Vercel. We're now in the phase of securing Canton DevNet access and integrating wallet providers for production.  Total output: 209 source files, 15,000 lines of Python, 13,000 lines of TypeScript, 2,500 lines of smart contracts, 32 documentation files, and 56 passing tests.  We didn't just build another Ethereum clone. We brought prediction markets to a chain that actually solves the problems Ethereum created. That's the opportunity.

Provide a summary of what your application will do:
We propose placing a lightweight on-ledger marker per validator to attest continued online presence. These markers create an immutable, time-stamped audit trail, enabling independent verification of validator uptime and supporting future compliance and 99% availability claims. Our monitoring client runs on the validator. It generates a tiny contract containing information about the validator, proving it is online. Our monitoring backend will be the 'observer' and show the validator status in a dashboard. Our backend is also able to send out alerts via webhooks to Slack.
Describe the expected users of your application:
Node as a Service providers, Super Validators, regular validators.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
Validators that use our solution install a script that publishes the contract every round. Transactions costs will therefore be paid by the validator. Our backend will perform read operations as observer to inspect the values of the contract, save it to a database, and display it in a dashboard. We will record the uptime of each validator every hour via a contract and create a marker. Number of transactions per customer: 168 (144 by the user, 24 by our backend).
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
Validators interact with the ledger by publishing a tiny contract every round. The monitoring backend will interact with the ledger by observing those contracts and by registering accumulated data as well as placing a marker.
Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.
Recording the accumulated data from all observed events (validator-published contracts).
Does this activity use Canton Coin or Activity Markers to generate rewards?
For each hourly contract that the backend publishes (CreateCommand) it places an activity marker in the same transaction (FeaturedAppRight_CreateActivityMarker).
On a per user basis, what is your expected daily number of transactions
24
Under what conditions may a user generate multiple transactions per round/epoch?
The user's transaction does not result in an FA marker. So the user may generate as many transactions as the user can pay for. The backend that creates a contract every hour and places a marker will never exceed 2

super linear

15.02.2026

Without FA status, obtaining validator node access becomes more difficult, which would delay Canton integration. However, the core business model is self-sustainable, the application already works with traditional s

What is CanTrustAI

Why the Canton chain

Non-custodial design
Users execute transactions from their own wallets and pay network fees directly. No custody, no pooled accounts

Describe the expected users of your application:
Small groups who want lightweight coordination and incentives. Examples include households, roommates, small teams, and community groups running recurring tasks.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
Primary use case is chore completion rewards. Does this activity use Canton Coin or Activity Markers to g

Hey everyone,
Couple of questions that came to mind.
A) Curious how the privacy feature works, would it be only for non-CC payments? As Canton Coin transfers are public.
B) A market for on-chain quests (where users can earn rewards for taking simple actions like connecting their wallet) seems to have the potential to generate another wave of FA markers from farming activity. Which, based on what's been going on in the last 2 weeks, I think we understand how problematic that can be.
I'm asking from a place of genuine curiosity, is this aligned with what the committee currently wants? Is the current strategy to incentivize onchain activity of any nature, or is the goal still to segment incentives based on tangible, long-term value contribution to the network?
Thanks
--
Juan Costa
COO

Hi Juan
Thanks for the questions
CC is intentionally designed to be public, transparent, and permissionless. Other assets in the network are free to choose the right amount of privacy for them. Privacy works via segmentation so you will have no visibility into any details of those assets (and don't today!)
On the tokenomics - reading between the lines a bit - I respectfully disagree with the categorization of the wallets and quests as not having tangible, long term value contribution. Onboarding a retail community is important, and very valuable, to the apps in the ecosystem. Having activated retail which knows how to connect, use the wallets, etc increases TAM for other apps. There is also an incredible about of work going on in the background to improve how the wallet experience works in Canton. The wallets you see today are involved very closely and working around the clock to help improve that for everyone. Early users go a long way to providing the right data to help tighten that up.
However, assume you are right for a moment - the tokenomics naturally allow for both (valuable, not valuable) without need to (overly) police behaviors.
Because subscriptions, quests, etc lose their economic viability at burn/mint equilibrium (BME), and price of CC should not go below inelastic (high utility) demand for the network (as that would make supply deflationary), there is nearly unlimited capacity for inelastic app rewards.
That being said, we are observing how markers are being used. There are some behaviors that may interfere with that natural equilibrium mechanism that we may ask FAs to avoid. Still doing my homework on this one. But, I will say, the apps that are doing quests and adding users to wallets don't appear to be interfering with the natural tokenomics and BME - it really is the use of markers that requires some additional thought.
At BME, the most important thing will be finding ways to get users to use the chain because what is being provided has sufficient utility. As BME certainly looks to be on the near horizon, It's a good time for everyone to shift focus in that direction.
On Fri, Oct 24, 2025 at 4:26 PM Juan  Juan via lists.sync.global <juan=noves.fi@...> wrote:
Hey everyone,
Couple of questions that came to mind.
A) Curious how the privacy feature works, would it be only for non-CC payments? As Canton Coin transfers are public.
B) A market for on-chain quests (where users can earn rewards for taking simple actions like connecting their wallet) seems to have the potential to generate another wave of FA markers from farming activity. Which, based on what's been going on in the last 2 weeks, I think we understand how problematic that can be.
I'm asking from a place of genuine curiosity, is this aligned with what the committee currently wants? Is the current strategy to incentivize onchain activity of any nature, or is the goal still to segment incentives based on tangible, long-term value contribution to the network?
Thanks
--
Juan C

Pasting Juan's response (at his request)
Hi Eric,
Thanks a lot for the quick and thorough response.
There's one thing that I don't quite understand from how you're thinking about this. Wanted to provide an example to clarify.
Let's say that I'm an FA and I sign an enterprise contract, to provide Canton-specific services to an institution that's coming to Canton and will be transacting onchain. My incentive structure for onboarding that institution currently consists of earning rewards with one CC transfer every 10 minutes, for the subscription billing.
If a wallet app allows a user to connect to Canton, by creating a new party in the wallet's server, and then starts airdropping CC rewards every 10 minutes to that user, then they're receiving the same amount of network incentives as the subscription billing for the institutional user.
If I follow your
BME framework, then I think both of these activities would be equally rewarded by the network? That would imply that they're both deemed to provide the same level of value. And that's the part that confuses me.
Certainly I'm not saying that wallets or onboarding retail is of no value. Wallet UX is for sure important, in any ecosystem. But I'm not sure I follow how we're categorizing or segmenting different "levels" of value then.
I was starting with the assumption that not all subscriptions / airdrops / quests, etc provide the same level of utility to the network. Which I think is a fair assumption to make, especially in light of previous experiences with airdrops / farming in other blockchain ecosystems. And therefore the incentive structure would recognize that somehow.
But I don't quite see how that would happen at
BME in the scenario you outlined, with the way FA incentives currently work. Totally possible there's a piece that I'm not understanding.
Thanks again. I think this is a very fruitful conversation to be having.
On Fri, Oct 24, 2025 at 4:53 PM Eric Saraniecki via lists.sync.global <eric=digitalasset.com@...> wrote:
Hi Juan
Thanks for the questions
CC is intentionally designed to be public, transparent, and permissionless. Other assets in the network are free to choose the right amount of privacy for them. Privacy works via segmentation so you will have no visibility into any details of those assets (and don't today!)
On the tokenomics - reading between the lines a bit - I respectfully disagree with the categorization of the wallets and quests as not having tangible, long term value contribution. Onboarding a retail community is important, and very valuable, to the apps in the ecosystem. Having activated retail which knows how to connect, use the wallets, etc increases TAM for other apps. There is also an incredible about of work going on in the background to improve how the wallet experience works in Canton. The wallets you see today are involved very closely and working around the clock to help improve that for everyone. Early users go a long way to providing the right data to help tigh

Thanks Juan
My opinions on this matter move with observing behaviors and I am by no means infallible on this topic but here is how I think about your question.
There is no value in debating the relative value of expanding the network. In all cases, if $1 spent returns > $1 of rewards, that is what I call elastic activity and really we should look at all of that equally. For you, might be more valuable to onboard a hedge fund, for someone else it might be more valuable to onboard 10k users. A rising tide floats all boats.
I think you are missing an important thing about BME - the network values both your examples equally
only
in elastic flows. Inelastic flows favor the institutional flows dramatically.
At BME, neither the Enterprise nor the Wallet apps continue the elastic flows. At that point, only inelastic flows are profitable. The enterprise app likely has much higher margins.
Imagine a scenario where there network is roughly in BME and a tx costs $1 in CC burn of network fees
AppA : provides $0.01 of Utility per transaction
App B : provides $1 of Utility per transaction
Flows for AppA will look like
User pays $1 to do tx on Network -> AppA earns $1 in CC -> AppA passes 99c of CC to user
Flows for AppB will look like
User pays $1 to do tx on Network -> AppB earns $1 in CC -> AppB keeps $1 of CC
Note that elastic flows cannot do what I described in these two scenarios because the User and the App 'work together' to spend $1 to earn > $1. In the scenarios above, the User net pays something (what I can inelastic). My expectation is that the enterprise apps will be able to extract (in absolute terms) higher unit costs per tx. At BME, you can think of App Rewards similar to interchange.
I, personally, am in favor of raising Synchronizer fees for this reason. It creates more room for margin when using the app without crowding out apps that can only exist at lower fee rates. This is unlike any other network where the network takes 100% of the network fees.

BME framework, then I think both of these act

Hello,
Here is an update from the FA requester:
toggle quoted message
Show quoted text
Thank you all for taking the time to review our submission and for the thoughtful discussion around the HandlPay FA proposal. I wanted to briefly address some of the points raised and provide additional context on how we’re approaching user incentives and privacy.
1.
⁠
⁠
Privacy Architecture
HandlPay’s privacy feature leverages the Canton Network’s segmented privacy model. As Eric noted, Canton Coin remains public by design, while our stablecoin payments (e.g. USDC) and user-linked metadata are handled within private subnets. This allows users to enjoy end-to-end privacy for their P2P transfers, while still maintaining compliance and auditability for regulated partners.
2.
⁠
⁠
Incentives & Preventing Farming Behavior
Our incentive structure focuses on authentic engagement, not pure activity volume. Each user must link a verified social media account or WhatsApp handle as part of their onboarding with HandlPay (= real users, minimizes likelihood of non-bona fide farming).
Rewarded actions (like wallet linking) are one-time or capped and tied to educational modules that guide users toward real usage
3.
⁠ Usage of Activity Markers
T
here will be no automatic "drips" or recurring airdrops of CC. Activity Markers will only be issued for real, verifiable user actions within the app (e.g. linking a wallet, completing a payment to a WhatsApp number, or engaging with a learning quest that can be tracked on-chain). When CC transfers occur, they will embed the FA markers directly in the transaction flow, ensuring that all rewarded actions correspond to legitimate, on-chain user behavior.

Below is an update on how they plan on using Activity Markers
About HandlPay
HandlPay enables freelancers, creators, and social-commerce entrepreneurs to receive payments using only their social media handles or messenger usernames. In 2025, we onboarded more than 320,000 social media accounts and reached 60,000 MAU. In January, we will join the Cointelegraph Accelerator and further position HandlPay as a social-media-native payments product with built-in transaction privacy.
Summary & future usage of FA markers
1-31 December 2025:
• Time-limited campaign focused on user signups, connecting Canton addresses to social media handles, and making payments to social media users
• No drip incentives or free rewards were distributed; users paid between $10 and $1,000 to participate
From 1 January 2026:
• Payments made to social media handles
• Bridging of assets from EVM to Canton Network
• Incentivising payments in USDCx
+
CC (no markers on CC if already marked as featured transfer)
Commitment to holding CC
(proposal)
:
• We will hold 80% of all rewards until HANDL is listed (January 2026)
• Following listing, we will hold 100% of rewards

messages.body:
New Featured App Submission Details
Submitted:
2025-12-23 21:39:24
Entry ID:
197
Email
austinmoothart@...
Name of applying institution
BitGo
Summary of Company and Background
BitGo provides the most secure and scalable solutions for the digital asset economy, offering regulated custody, staking and trading, and core infrastructure to investors and builders alike.   Founded in 2013 — the early days of crypto — BitGo pioneered the multi-signature wallet and later built TSS to improve upon other companies’ MPC offerings. Between multi-sig and TSS, BitGo offers the safest technology on the market and safeguards over 1400+ tokens across a wide variety of blockchains.   Over the years, BitGo has expanded from offering wallets into providing a full-suite solution that lets clients hold assets safely and then put them to work. BitGo launched BitGo Trust Company in 2018, providing fully regulated, qualified cold storage to complement BitGo Inc’s original hot wallet solution.   In 2020, BitGo launched BitGo Prime, which allows its clients to trade, borrow, and lend. Moreover, BitGo also provides access to DeFi, staking, NFT wallets, and beyond. Additionally, BitGo launched for WBTC, aka wrapped Bitcoin   Today, BitGo is one of the largest independent qualified custodians with over $90B+ in assets under custody, multiple regulated entities across the globe and a proven leader in digital asset security, custody, and liquidity. BitGo provides the operational backbone for more than 4500+ institutional clients in over 100+ countries — a list that includes many regulated entities and the world’s top cryptocurrency exchanges and platforms. BitGo also processes approximately 8% of all global Bitcoin transactions by value.
Name of the application:
Qualified Custodyt
URL of the applying institution
https://www.bitgo.com
Product Website
https://app.bitgo.com/
Emails for Responsible Persons
austinmoothart@...
Link to Brand Materials (optional)
https://design.bitgo.com/
Provide a summary of what your application will do:
Qualified Custody Wallet for Canton Coin and other Canton Token Standard digital assets in the Canton Network such as USDCx and CBTC.
Describe the expected users of your application:
Institutional - This covers those looking to deploy capital and includes Hedge Funds, Asset Managers, Venture Capital firms, OTC / Market Makers, Family Offices, Endowments, Pension Funds, RIA and 401K and UHNW investors.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
Enable users to transact on Canton Network out of Qualified Cold Custodial wallets that enable the users to be compliant with regulatory oversight.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
Deposit, transfer, withdrawal of Canton digital assets with key generated and stored offline in cold storage.
Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.
Client transactions to deposit

topic.title: New Featured App Request: Validity.id Inc.

messages.body:
New Featured App Submission Details
Submitted:
2025-12-01 18:33:44
Entry ID:
125
Email
Michael@...
Name of applying institution
Validity.id Inc.
Summary of Company and Background
Validity brings cryptographic authenticity to digital information. Organizations use Validity to sign data and publish a verifiable record that can be checked by anyone through a Registry form embedded on their website. Each verification queries the blockchain for a matching identifier, ensuring the data is tamper-evident and traceable to its source. Founded in 2023, Validity is already trusted by institutions including Rice University, MIT Sloan School of Management, Duke University, the University of Texas at Tyler, and others, shaping a new standard for publishing trustworthy, verifiable information online.
Name of the application:
Validity Registry
URL of the applying institution
https://validity.id/
Product Website
https://validity.id/#benefits
Emails for Responsible Persons
Michael@...
, dean@...
Party ID for the Featured Application
InformationSystemsLTD-validator-1::1220d572036d26e3f8ef2fe127184d812a00e0b1b36e129acf990a82a06651e735a1
Provide a summary of what your application will do:
Validity offers a seamless path for organizations to publish cryptographically authenticated data, instantly verified through an embedded Registry form that checks each claim against its blockchain identifier. Just as blockchain adoption made money move without intermediaries, Validity enables trust to flow without third parties.
Describe the expected users of your application:
Validity is designed for organizations that issue information requiring public trust—such as universities, financial institutions, and associations—and for any stakeholder who needs to verify that information directly from its source.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
Regardless of the schema type—degree, enrollment record, transcript, or file upload—Validity creates a transaction on the Canton Network whenever an institution publishes a new attestation. These transactions are settled using Canton Coin as the fee token. Verification events initiated through the embedded Registry form represent measurable network usage; these events are tracked and can be represented as Activity Markers within Canton’s Activity Markets to qualify for application rewards.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
Validity issues cryptographically signed attestations—such as degrees, enrollment records, and transcripts—currently used across five universities. On Canton Network, Validity would anchor these attestations to Canton’s privacy-enabled, interoperable infrastructure. Each attestation published through Validity can be written to Canton,  creating a tamper-evident, permissioned record that institutions control and external verifiers can trust.
Describe the activities that your application will earn applicatio

Validity is wanting to add to their application some more information:
toggle quoted message
Show quoted text
Thank you again for outlining the requirements for Featured Applications.
I have seen recent notes in the sync group regarding Featured App status being revoked when usage patterns do not align with current guidance. With that in mind, I wanted to proactively clarify one aspect of Validity Registry’s planned Canton Network deployment to avoid any ambiguity as we move toward activation.
Validity is porting its entire application to the Canton Network as its primary production ledger. As part of this transition, our existing institutional customers will be re-issuing historical graduate credentials on Canton so that all active academic attestations reside on the same on-chain infrastructure going forward.
This re-issuance is:
Performed only by authenticated university registrars already using Validity in production
Limited to previously issued, institution-owned academic records
A one-time transition step, not an ongoing issuance pattern
Operationally necessary to ensure continuity, auditability, and verifier trust once Canton is the system of record
Following this migration, issuance activity will return to its normal fall, spring, and summer term-based cadence, and verification activity will continue to reflect user-initiated credential checks through our embedded Registry forms.
To ensure we are aligned with Canton’s expectations, could you advise on how this one-time historical re-issuance is typically classified for Featured Application monitoring and rewards purposes? We want to make sure our deployment and reporting are structured appropriately from the outset.
For completeness, I want to note a correction from earlier discussions:
our SV sponsor is Digital Assets.
We are happy to answer any questions in advance. Wishing you a wonderful holiday season!
All the best,
Michael

This is a contract on the canton network that represents a license to use a WHOLE agent or certain aspects of an agent
3. The application will also interact directly with the cantara billing utility for subscription billing

UPCOMING
Agent Ownership and Usage Tokens: As organizations increasingly register ownership and usage tokens ON canton this will generate activity and associated application rewards. I.e. the registration itself.
FUTURE
Future Possibility Open for discussion

Below is the update from Orphil LLC (Agentic Ledger)
_____________________________________________
I have explicitly changed the following question to remove and reference to reads or compliance queries as that is not part of the plan. In today
 world we would interact with cantara billing application.
This question is updated in the below:
Does this activity use Canton Coin or Activity Markers to generate rewards?:
Yes IN THE FUTURE. Activity markers will be tied to transaction types: ownership token creation, usage token issuance. There would be NO activity markers for reads
Full application updated below. only that specific question changed.
New Featured App Submission Details
Name of Applying Institution:
Orphil LLC (Agentic Ledger)
Name of the application::
Agentic Ledger
URL of the applying institution::
www.agenticledger.ai
Emails for Responsible Persons::
ore@...

Hello,
Ore wanted to add a little more information at the request of a committee member. Please see below:
useful description of AgenticLedger's use case. Please note that the below is adjusted to reflect some insights he provided on maximizing value producing vs value extractive activity.
Our approach
Annual Subscription:
Following the model of some other more mature platforms in the ecosystem we will be applying an Annual subscription based on a contract for access to tokenize AI agents (today) and other assets (in future). There are no markers involved in this. There may be rewards activity resulting from the actual payment of this annual subscription which we would apply a reward share to.
Customer Resell Revenue Share: Our users are able to sell agents that they create to others. The creator of the agent will keep 70% and the platform 30% (can be negotiated).
Note that this is NOT on chain and does not involve rewards or markers.
Usage: When a customer uses an AI agent it costs Tokens from providers like openai, anthropic etc etc.
This usage will NOT be the subject of rewards or markers.
Instead we will explore leverage the new token standard such that a customer can buy X number of tokens and then spend the tokens on usage. Note we have extremely limited knowledge of how the new token standard works so this will be a research process for us to build out this capability over time
In summary
There would no longer any markers involved in our offerings (not for usage or activity or subscriptions) although revenue share on rewards would be applicable to the annual subscription mentioned in #1
For more general context
What does agenticledger do?
We run a platform that allows any organization to:
A. Create a number of AI agents (based on subscription)
B. Register ownership of those AI agents to a Registered Owner and a Licensed Owner
C. Rent out / License out those agents to others including on chain registration of the subscription license
Useful agents and agent capabilities we have built in the ecosystem
- Canty: ecosystem expert
- Nodefortress agent
- Agent with ability to obtain CC pricing from Kaiko
- Agent with ability to respond to DAML related questions based on the DA DAML trainig
- Agent with ability to obtain Feature app
- Agent with abilty to obtain CC metrics from theTie
- Agent with ability to obtain from CCview
- [In progress] Working with pixelplex Agents that have Canton wallet
- [In progress] Agentic Portal where agent created data can be dashboarded and natural language queried by any user
- Agents with ability to provide alerting on certain specific on chain metrics
- etc...
The above approach was adjusted based on feedback from earlier conversations but i am happy to show up if needed to have a deeper discussion, answer any questions and open to adjust further... My ideal timing would be in the new year due to travel/holidays but i want to respect any need for more urgent conversation and will show up to any conversation for

Summary of Company and Background
Silvana is a Web3 platform focused on privacy and zero-knowledge proofs, providing an agentic environment for real-world provable data, tokenization, and on-chain asset trading.
Name of the application:
Silvana Book
URL of the applying institution
https://silvana.one
Product Website
https://silvana.one
