Party ID for the Featured Application
silvana-orderbook::1220997446016f1e96be9215bab224eace372752853ef99175c332307489bccbb07b
Provide a summary of what your application will do:
Silvana Book enables private, ultra-fast off-chain order matching with parties’ complete asset control and secure atomic on-chain settlement on the Canton blockchain.  Silvana enables agentic trading and settlement.  Proving agents can prove aggregated trading data, such as average daily prices, using zero-knowledge circuits without disclosing private transaction information.
Describe the expected users of your application:
Retail and Institutional Users, Application Teams, Business Analysts, Explorers, Developers.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
Silvana Book will settle all trades on the Canton Network using the DVP (Delivery vs. Payment) flow for CIP-56 tokens.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
On-chain activity consists of transactions executed via DVP flow for CIP-56 tokens on Canton.
Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.
Subscription payments, Activity Markers on DVP transactions.
Does this activity use Canton Coin or Activity Markers to generate rewards?
Subscription payments will be in Canton Coin; no Activity Markers will be attached. DVP transactions will have one Activity Marker attached.
On a per user basis, what is your expected daily number of transactions
For DVP, we expect 0-10 transactions per day per regular user, depending on user activity. For subscriptions, we expect 4 transactions per day, which will be decreased to 1 transaction per day according to the schedule approved by the Tokenomics committee.
Under what conditions may a user generate multiple transactions per round/epoch?
In case the user trades in different pairs within a 10-minute window, it can generate several transactions in the same round.
How do you expect your transactions to scale as your customer base scales? E.g. Linearly, Super Linear, Sub Linear.
Linearly
What is your anticipated launch date on MainNet?
5 Jan 2026
Who will be your first customers and what is the expected go-live dates?
The first customers will be Loop wallet users, and the go-live date is 5 Jan 2026.
How would not having FA status change your operating plans?
Featured App status is essential to scale our application, integrate more CIP

-

Demo Video 16:9 ratio (optional)

Provide a summary of what your application will do:
The application is already live without the FA status. It offers the conversion between stablecoins, tokenized demand deposits, tokenized money market funds, etc., and fiat at par value directly with the respective issuer, rather than through an exchange.
Describe the expected users of your application:
The users of the application are: Issuers and Liquidity providers of the respective assets, settlement venues, as well as receiving institutions (i.e., Banks and PSPs).
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
In the first instance, the Ubyx Clearing App uses Canton for cooridination purposes. The assets do not have to live on Canton. However, there are certain benefits/simplifications if the assets are on Canton directly. Furthermore, users that are already on canton do not have to integrate into our standard API but can rather interact with our application through daml.
Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.
The activities that earn rewards are the standard processes that we offer.
What is your anticipated launch date on MainNet?
We have been live since 20
Who will be your first customers and what is the expected go-live dates?
We are already live, working with
How would not having FA status change your operating plans?
We might have to do more of the processing off-chain to keep to cost down. This is a low margin use case, so we can't have high transaction costs.
Does your application have any controls to prevent non-bona fide transactions?
The participants of the application are undergoing a KYB process and are regulated institutions. The activity markers are generated on the transactions that govern the redemptions/conversions themselves, and are involving multiple of these regulated parties.
Additional Notes for the Committee’s consideration:
So far we have been running the application without an FA status. We are not expecting a large reward generation, but would like to keep the transaction cost down.
Note:
Next step is to have them demo the App at the next meeting. Any questions or concerns before proceeding?

Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.
The activities that earn rewards are the standard processes that we offer like: Redemptions or minting of Stablecoins, TDDs, or Tokenized Money Market Funds. Provide a summary of what your application will do:
Cansai is the first fully native iOS wallet built specifically for the Canton Network. It enables users to securely store, manage, and interact with multiple Canton Network assets through a seamless, intuitive mobile experience. Designed with security, privacy, and usability at its core, Cansai is a non-custodial wallet that gives users full ownership and control of their private keys while leveraging Apple’s ecosystem for native performance and secure key synchronization across devices.  The application supports a wide range of Canton Network assets, including Canton Coin, CBTC, USDCx, and others, allowing users to manage all their holdings in one place. With thoughtfully crafted UI/UX, native iOS integration, and 24/7 in-app chat support, Cansai lowers the barrier to entry for both new and experienced users and provides a reliable, user-friendly gateway to decentralized finance on the Canton Network.
Describe the expected users of your application:
Cansai is intended for iOS users interacting with the Canton Network who need a secure, non-custodial wallet to manage Canton-native assets. This includes both retail and professional users such as ecosystem participants, developers, validators, and early adopters - as well as new users seeking a simple, well-designed mobile experience. Cansai is particularly suited for security-conscious users who value full control over their assets, strong privacy, and seamless integration with Apple’s ecosystem.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
Cansai interacts with the Canton Network as a strictly non-custodial, client-side wallet. All private keys are generated, stored, and used exclusively on the user’s iOS devices. Transactions are signed locally on the device and are never exposed to our infrastructure. A Tenkai-operated validator participant acts only as a relayer, validating signatures and routing pre-signed transactions to the Canton Network without custody of user as

Provide a summary of what your application will do:
Cantoons is an on-chain game where players engage in battles against alien enemies and to earn limited rare in-game diamonds. Each gameplay session is started and finalized by submitting a dedicated on-chain transaction that records game results on the Canton ledger. All the users’ scores will be recorded in the leaderboard, a big part of the all players will be getting rewards based on their scores/rare in-game items they collect. (might add a few extra tx-s to every game session on game start, rare diamond collection after Canton team’s approval)   P.S  Please note that the application is in testnet mode and right now has guest mode only, however we will be adding the following features on Mainnet: 1) Auth Mode: Transactions will be made from users' unique wallets using auto-approval without interrupting the gameplay. Every users will have its own score bound to his wallet   2) Energy capacity and boosters to limit number of tx-s per user per day (will make it hard to cheat by giving bonuses to the old wallets)   3) After a call and the team approval the plan is to add Characters shop with improved traits to score higher in the leaderboard (we will add twitter connect and make tweet/retweet about Canton and the game necessary to get the new character)   4) Improved security for the dropping diamonds to prevent cheating on the front end
Describe the expected users of your application:
The application targets Web3 gamers interested in skill-based, play-to-earn-style experiences, crypto-native users exploring on-chain applications, and casual players seeking competitive gameplay with leaderboards and periodic reward distributions.   Our team has our own 2000+ users media and lots of connection to CIS and Western influencers, so we might add more users to the ones currently exploring Canton ecosystem

Provide a summary of what your application will do:
CC Thank You is a lightweight social micro-payment tool like Venmo designed to make appreciation easy and joyful on the Canton Network. It enables users to send small “thank you” payments to one another using Canton Coin. The focus is high-frequency, user-driven interactions that create genuine network utility without requiring complex financial workflows.
Describe the expected users of your application:
CC Thank You allows users to send single-tap micro-tips to express gratitude, support creators, reward helpfulness, or recognize contributions. Each “thank you” is a real Canton Coin transfer between users, optionally paired with a “Thank You Activity Marker” to reflect meaningful engagement. The app provides a simple, intuitive interface for expressing appreciation on-chain.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
Expected users include:  Everyday Canton users who want to send appreciation  Community members rewarding helpful contributions  Educators, creators, and volunteers receiving small tips  Teams and groups that want a lightweight recognition tool CC Thank You is designed for broad, general-purpose use.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
Writes  Submit a CC transfer between sender and recipient  Optionally call the Featured App API to create an Activity Marker  Reads  Query prior thank-you events  Verify balances before sending  All transactions are explicitly initiated and signed by the user.
Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.
CC Thank You earns rewards from:  Canton Coin transfers created when users send tips  Optional Thank You Activity Markers that represent genuine social interactions  Sticker-style “gratitude tokens” that trigger micro-transfers  Group appreciation moments where multiple users independently send thank-you events
Does this activity use Canton Coin or Activity Markers to generate rewards?
CC transfers are the core action  Activity Markers are added only for meaningful user-driven appreciation events
On a per user basis, what is your expected daily number of transactions
Typical users: 3–20 thank-you transactions per day  Active creators or community moderators: 20–50 per day

That allows users to interact with our own token standard registry.  Token Issuance: Enterprises can create custom tokens with full metadata (name, symbol, description) Token Management: Complete lifecycle management including minting, burning, and transfers CIP-0056 Compliance: Full implementation of Canton Token Standard interfaces (MetadataV1, HoldingV1, TransferFactory, BurnMintFactory, Allocation) Atomic Settlement: Support for Delivery-vs-Payment (DvP) transactions via the Allocation interface Wallet Interoperability: Tokens are compatible with Splice Wallet and other Canton applications
Describe the expected users of your application:
Enterprise Token Issuers Financial institutions creating digital representations of assets Corporations issuing loyalty points, reward tokens, or internal currencies Fund managers tokenizing fund shares or investment units B2B Clients Companies needing white-label token issuance capabilities Payment processors requiring Canton-compliant token infrastructure Trading platforms needing atomic settlement capabilities End Users (via B2B clients) Retail investors holding tokenized assets Corporate employees receiving token-based compensation Consumers earning and redeeming loyalty tokens
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and Activity Markets for application rewards?
Use Case 1: Token Transfers Transfers exercise the TokenHolding_Transfer choice, archiving the sender’s holding and creating new holdings for the receiver. For economically meaningful transfers, we issue a FeaturedAppActivityMarker, which becomes an AppRewardCoupon and contributes to rewards.  Use Case 2: Token Minting Minting creates new TokenHolding contracts. Since minting reflects real economic value creation, we generate Activity Markers for these events, which are converted into AppRewardCoupons by Super Validators. We will record transactions to the ledger for our user's interactions with assets.
Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.
Esoteric flows that don't currently provide a way to include a CC transfer to pay for traffic costs
Does this activity use Canton Coin or Activity Markers to generate rewards?
We will pass through fees on CC transfers and CIP-56 transfer right off the jump but cover expenses on esoteric flows via activity markers
On a per user basis, what is your expected daily number of transactions
We don’t have an estimate.
Under what conditions may a user generate multiple transactions per round/epoch?
We don’t control any timing on user transactions. Initially we expect them to transact with Canton Coin and other Token Standard transactions. There is no incentive for the users to generate transaction volume.
How do you expect your transactions to scale as your customer base scales? E.g. Linearly, Super Linear, Sub Linear.
Linearly
What is your anticipated launch date on MainNet?
2025-12-11
Who will be your first customers and what is the expected go-live dates?
We have a wait list of thousands of retail users waiting to be onboarded to use our Wallet.
How would not having FA status change your operating plans?
Esoteric flows traffic costs would have to be paid by us until we implement custom daml to charge users CC on every transaction.
Does your application have any controls to prevent non-bona fide transactions?
Our wallet API includes rate limiting to prevent users from any type of spam transactio

messages.body:
New Featured App Request: FDF Technologies
Email
info@...
Name of applying institution
FDF Technologies
Summary of Company and Background
FDF Technologies is a product-driven company that develops and operates its own proprietary
applications within the Canton ecosystem. Leveraging our deep expertise in staking
infrastructure, we build secure, high-performing products designed to close critical market gaps
and drive network adoption. We own our product suite, allowing us to align our long-term
success directly with the growth of the Canton network.
Name of the application:
Vala Wallet: Canton Non Custodial Web Wallet with Featured App Integrations
URL of the applying institution
vala-wallet.cc
Emails for Responsible Persons
daniel@...
; lucas@...
Party ID for the Featured Application
ValaWallet-validator-1::12201339973af7bb4049bfff4046da2bd22be98176cfc9011617840684bc2
ef781d4
Provide a summary of what your application will do:
Vala Wallet is a strictly non-custodial web wallet that serves as the primary gateway to the
Canton ecosystem. It allows users to generate and store keys client-side (via passkeys,
hardware modules, or local KMS). The wallet provides seamless access to the full spectrum of
"Allfi" apps including Featured Apps, subscriptions, DEXs, lending, and broader DeFi utilizing
our own Wallet Connect SDK or direct payload integrations. It facilitates client-side signing
where the private key never leaves the device, while the validator participant acts only to
validate signatures and route transactions, ensuring users keep full control while accessing the
broad ecosystem.
Describe the expected users of your application:
The expected users are everyone seeking a clean non-custodial UX who wish to maintain full
control of their keys while accessing DeFi protocols, automated market makers, and lending
markets.
How will your application interact with the Canton Network and leverage Canton Coin and
Activity Markets for application rewards?
Interaction with the Ledger Vala functions as a strictly non-custodial gateway where private keys
remain client-side and never leave the user's device. Users sign transactions locally (offline),
and our validator participant acts solely as a relayer to validate these signatures and route the
pre-signed transactions to the Canton Network. Leveraging Rewards (Use Case: Traffic Cost
Funding) We leverage Featured App reward markers by attaching them to complex user
transactions (such as app integrations or subscriptions). These rewards are generated
specifically to fund the traffic costs on the validator, allowing us to incentivize deeper user
interaction with the network. Note: We explicitly do not attach markers to simple transactions
that automatically generate rewards, nor do we mark drip-style campaigns.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
Key Management: Private keys remain client-side; keys are created and encrypted in the user’s
environment. Signing: The user signs transaction

WebWise::12208e1d1e585578692037e8e049616903294b480314e27ab29040bd1e50789a21a9
Provide a summary of what your application will do:
Canton Predict is a decentralized prediction-market platform built on the Canton Network, enabling users to trade on the outcomes of future crypto events using DAML contracts synchronized via the Global Synchronizer (GS). Every prediction market is represented as a DAML template containing two key states: open market and resolved outcome. When users create markets, place positions, or settle payouts, each action triggers a transaction recorded and validated through GS, resulting in Canton Coin burn proportional to the market’s usage volume. The platform combines financial utility (hedging, speculation, forecasting) with strong on-ledger transparency and deterministic settlement — ideal for the Canton economic model.
Describe the expected users of your application:
Primary users: Institutional and retail forecasters, data analysts, crypto traders, and enterprises using prediction markets for probabilistic forecasting.   Secondary users: Application developers embedding Canton Predict APIs for event-based financial products (insurance, derivatives, and structured outcomes). Research institutions and auditors verifying prediction outcomes via the public We expect an early community of data-driven participants migrating from existing Web3 markets like Polymarket and Zeitgeist.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.
Canton Predict will run entirely on DAML smart contracts deployed to the Canton ledger. Each market event corresponds to a unique Canton template that tracks: Market creation Position opening and resolution Settlement payouts Transactions are propagated via Global Synchronizer, ensuring atomic settlement between opposing positions (YES/NO, UP/DOWN). Each step (open, close, resolve) involves Canton Coin micro-burn, and the GS ensures ordering, validation, and distribution of mint rewards accordingly.
Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.
Canton Predict will earn rewards from user transaction fees denominated in USD and settled through Canton Coins. Each prediction market cycle (creation → trading → settlement) generates measurable usage volume and MB load, which contribute directly to the application reward pool. Specifically: Market creation fee (burn CC) Trading fee (burn CC per trade) Settlement fee (burn CC when outcome resolved)
Does this activity use Canton Coin or Activity Markers to generate rewards?
Canton Coin (CC)

Summary of Company and Background
Axymos Systems Limited builds core usability infrastructure for the Canton Network, with a focus on identity, naming and resolution services that make institutional blockchains easier and safer to use. The team combines backgrounds in capital markets, digital asset infrastructure, and enterprise software. Axymos’ goal is to provide neutral, shared “plumbing” that multiple wallets, custodians, and applications can rely on, rather than vertically integrated end-user products.
Name of the application:
xNS
URL of the applying institution
https://xns.axymos.dev/
Product Website
https://xns.axymos.dev/
Emails for Responsible Persons
dave@...
;

Party ID for the Featured Application
auth0_007c691c4d28726455d23d1aa6dd::122046d30acaaa01b3a0aae069cbfb5983660360af866dd76b335570acacb2b31c3a
URL for the public code repository (if available)
No public repository at launch.
Demo Video 16:9 ratio (optional)
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1AGiTBn8qIKKtemF6N3ov3PsdxZXsXzCG/view?usp=drive_link;
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1uXb8sd1FfVNCSZ9caUQmFqdtBHAPeDh1/view?usp=drive_link
Provide a summary of what your application will do:
xNS is the naming and routing layer for Canton. Instead of copying opaque Canton party IDs, users and institutions use names like alice.canton or desk1.fx.bankx.canton. Wallets and apps call xNS to turn those names into the correct Canton party and routing metadata before a transaction is sent. Relationship to CNS (and why xNS is not a CNS wrapper) CNS is the canonical, low-level party registry on Canton: “which legal/technical entity does this party represent?” xNS is an application-level naming and routing fabric: “what should this name mean today across wallets, desks, products and policies?” xNS uses CNS as an input but it is not a 1:1 alias or UI skin over CNS MVP (Loop + ENS connectivity via MetaMask) In the MVP, we have integrated with our partners at 5N Loop. A Loop user can: Claim a .canton name  In Loop, a user searches for an available name (e.g. alice.canton), claims it, and sets a default destination (their Canton wallet). Policy checks and a reserved list apply.   Send to names, not parties  When anyone types alice.canton in Loop, the wallet calls xNS, which returns the current Canton party and routing metadata (derived from CNS where relevant). The user only sees and approves alice.canton.   Link a .canton name to ENS via MetaMask  The user connects an Ethereum wallet (e.g. via MetaMask), proves control of an ENS name like alice.eth, and links it to alice.canton.   Respect reserved and sensitive names  xNS enforces a reserved list and simple policies so obvious trademarks, existing institutional names and abusive terms are not available through the standard retail flow. These are handled via controlled enterprise or manual processes.

topic.title: New Featured App Request: ALUM LABS

messages.body:
New Featured App Submission Details
Name of Applying Institution:
ALUM LABS
Name of the application::
Canton Monitor
URL of the applying institution::
https://www.alumlabs.io/
Emails for Responsible Persons::
alex@...
jan@...
Party ID for the Featured Application::
alumlabs-mainnet-1::1220e791a2e8606793f51aa04c0d5d59a837a040917f402114529f59b0085dc448d1
URL for the public code repository (if available)::
https://www.cantonmonitor.com/
Provide a summary of what your application will do::
Canton Monitor allows node operators to monitor their validator(s). Canton Monitor provides insights into how the hardware is running, checks what version of splice-node is used, sends an alert via Telegram bot if the validator is underperforming, and also sends a daily balance update based on Party ID.
Describe the expected users of your application::
Canton Monitor serves two types of users: -Node operators: Gain access to an automated alerting system that informs an operator of performance issues directly via a Telegram bot. Institutions: Will be able to look under the hood and better understand how their branded validator is performing. We also aim to make large institutions feel more comfortable with branding a validator which is run by a third party by providing insights and alerting them in case something is wrong.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.:
When Canton Monitor notifies a validator of bad performance (node offline for example), alongside the Telegram notification an activity marker is placed. 
When Canton Monitor notifies a validator of its daily Canton Coin balance (based on Party ID), alongside the Telegram notification an activity marker is placed.
Every 15min, Canton Monitor will record the overall uptime/performance of all registered validators and publish it on its website. An activity marker is placed when this happens.
Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.:
the application will only earn rewards as a featured app by using Activity Markers which are placed when notifications are sent out.
Does this activity use Canton Coin or Activity Markers to generate rewards?:
As Canton Monitor is built to support the Canton ecosystem, the service is provided free of charge to node operators. Canton Monitor uses metrics directly obtained from a validator and does not require any on-chain data. Therefore, Canton Monitor aims to use Activity Markers to register usage per validator and get rewarded as a featured app.
On a per user basis, what is your expected daily number of transactions:
-Per user of our platform, a minimum of 1 transaction is expected (daily balance notification). 
-More than 1 transaction is possible if the validator of the user goes offline. 
-One transaction in total (not per user) is generated to record the overall status of onboarded validators.
Under what conditions may a user generate multiple transactions per round/epoch?:

Adding full text from the attached PDF, to allow permanent links from onchain governance voting history:
Name of Applying Institution: ALUM LABS
Name of the application: Canton Monitor
URL of the applying institution:
https://www.alumlabs.io
Emails for Responsible Persons: alex@... ,
jan@...
Party ID for the Featured Application: alumlabs-mainnet-1::1220e791a2e8606793f51aa04c0d5d59a837a040917f402114529f59b0085dc448d1
URL for the public code repository (if available):
https://www.cantonmonitor.com
Provide a summary of what your application will do:
Canton Monitor provides validator node operators and institutions with real-time performance monitoring, automated alerting, and daily balance insights. It improves operational efficiency, increases transparency, and helps establish a standard for uptime monitoring across the Canton ecosystem. By offering both free and advanced tiers, it ensures broad adoption while also addressing emerging regulatory requirements — such as the SEC’s expectations that asset managers monitor uptime and availability of the infrastructure they rely on. Describe the expected users of your application: Node operators who need real-time alerts to quickly address downtime or performance issues. Institutions and asset managers who require transparency into validator uptime and balance growth over time to build trust and meet compliance obligations.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger:
Each validator onboarded through Canton Monitor will be enrolled in a Cantara subscription. Alum Labs will lock Canton tokens per validator to fund the free base tier. This generates on-chain subscription activity, while optional advanced tiers allow users to lock their own tokens to unlock more frequent or customized alerts.
Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from:
Application rewards will come from the Cantara subscription activity tied to each onboarded validator. Alum Labs funds the base subscriptions by locking tokens, and users can optionally engage in additional activity by locking their own tokens for premium features.
Does this activity use Canton Coin or Activity Markers to generate rewards?
Canton Coin.
On a per user basis, what is your expected daily number of transactions:
Approximately 24 transactions per validator per day (once per hour for free subscriptions). This includes subscription maintenance, continuous development, uptime monitoring and balance updates. For paid customers, it would be once per 10 minutes, 144 per day.
Under what conditions may a user generate multiple transactions per round/epoch?
Normally a validator with a free subscription will generate 24 transactions per day. If users subscribe to enriched monitoring or custom metrics, additional subscription interactions may occur — but these remain predictable and low-frequency. There are no conditions that a user should have multiple transactions per round.
How do you expect your transactions to scale

Hello,
Since ALUM Labs is moving away from Cantara due to its limitations for their use case, they have put together a clear explanation of iCanMonitor to help the committee understand the mechanics behind thier Featured App.
Please see attached and below
iCanMonitor – Explanation
Overview
iCanMonitor participates in the Featured App reward program by producing
economically meaningful and verifiable monitoring activity
that improves network health and validator reliability. Each activity is anchored on chain and connected to reward-eligible markers in a way that ensures integrity, auditability, and compatibility with FeaturedAppRight incentives.
Activity Definition
iCanMonitor generates a
distinct monitoring event
whenever certain validator-relevant backend operations occur, including:
●
Validator health changes (healthy
→
unhealthy, unhealthy
→
healthy)
●
Delivery of operator alerts
●
Daily balance collection and reporting
●
Snapshot creation and ingestion
●
Additional periodic health checks across the validator ecosystem
Each of these operations represents
real economic contribution
: improving validator uptime, increasing reliability, and providing data that benefits both operators and the overall network.
Hash-Anchored Event Proof
For every monitoring event, iCanMonitor generates a
16-byte event hash
. This hash is:
●
deterministic for the event
●
collision-resistant
●
unforgeable once anchored on Canton
●
compact and privacy-preserving
The hash acts as a
cryptographic proof-of-action
: a low-cost way to notarize backend activity without exposing operational data.
The hash is stored in a tiny on-chain contract (
ICanMonitorHashRecord
) that serves as a
canonical anchor
, allowing later verification that:
●
the event actually happened,
●
the event was produced by the iCanMonitor backend,
●
the associated activity marker was justified.
Reward-Relevant Activity Marker
In the
same atomic transaction
where the hash is anchored, the system exercises:
FeaturedAppRight_CreateActivityMarker
This ties
reward eligibility
directly to
verifiable, timestamped operational activity
. Key properties:
●
1:1 linkage
between hash-anchored operations and activity markers
●
No possibility of empty/fake marker emissions
●
Events must exist before markers can be created
●
Markers reflect the true economic value contributed by iCanMonitor
Economic Alignment
This design aligns tightly with the tokenomics goals of the Featured Apps program:
●
Integrity:
each reward claim corresponds to an independently verifiable operational event.
●
Fairness:
no marker can be emitted without actual service contribution.
●
Traceability:
every activity marker can be linked to a specific backend event via its on-chain hash.
●
Censorship-resistance:
markers and hashes are jointly finalized on Canton.
●
Incentive alignment:
iCanMonitor is rewarded proportionally to real monitoring value delivered to the ecosystem.
Summary for Tokenomics Committee
iCanMonitor’s Featured App integration guar

Hello,
Alum has updated the FA since AKASEC is moving away from Cantara due to its limitations for our use case, They hve put together a clear explanation of 'AKASCAN' to help the committee understand the mechanics behind our Featured App.
Please see attached and below
AKASCAN – Explanation
Overview
AKASCAN is a Featured App that strengthens the Canton ecosystem by performing
continuous vulnerability scanning, security posture analysis, and asset integrity tracking
. It participates in the Featured App reward mechanism by producing verifiable security events that materially improve ecosystem resilience.
Activity Definition
AKASCAN emits a verifiable event whenever its backend performs security-critical operations, including:
●
Continuous vulnerability scanning
of online assets (domains, endpoints, validators)
●
Periodic snapshotting
of backend statistics and risk metrics
●
Exposure monitoring
(e.g., new open ports, expired certificates, misconfigurations)
●
Risk-score recalculations
based on observed changes in an organization’s attack surface
●
Security-risk lifecycle events
, such as:
○
the detection of a new CVE relevant to a monitored asset
○
the discovery of a configuration change
○
or the removal/mitigation of a previously detected risk
These events reflect
true economic contribution
: reducing ecosystem-wide risk, identifying vulnerabilities early, and helping maintain a secure environment for validators and organizations.
Hash-Anchored Security Event Proof
For each security-relevant backend action, AKASCAN computes a
16-byte event hash
. This hash is anchored on-chain via an
AkascanHashRecord
which encodes:
●
the hash,
●
the timestamp,
●
the provider (AKASEC),
●
and nothing else (preserving operational confidentiality).
This design provides:
●
cryptographically anchored evidence
of security analysis activity
●
proof of execution of vulnerability scans
●
tamper-resistant chaining between backend events and reward eligibility
●
future verifiability for audits or programmatic inspection
Reward-Relevant Activity Marker
In the
same atomic transaction
that anchors the hash, AKASCAN exercises:
FeaturedAppRight_CreateActivityMarker
This ensures:
●
Every marker reflects a real, anchored security event
●
Markers cannot be “free-minted” without work
●
Rewards correspond to measurable security contributions
By design, the atomic composition ensures:
●
no activity marker exists without a corresponding hash
●
no hash exists without a corresponding marker
●
the economic reward is tightly coupled to verifiable security value
Economic Alignment
AKASCAN’s approach embodies the intent of Featured App tokenomics:
●
Security value
→
Reward value
●
Proof of scanning
→
Proof of contribution
●
Attack surface reduction
→
Reward generation
Specifically:
●
The ecosystem benefits from continuous, automated vulnerability detection.
●
AKASCAN benefits from rewards whenever it provides security value.
●
The incentive mechanism reinforces security monitoring as a sustai

Summary of Company and Background
Plume is a public, EVM-compatible blockchain built for the next evolution of Real World Assets (RWAs). We’re not just tokenizing assets — we’re creating a seamless way to use them like crypto: stake, swap, lend, borrow, loop, and more. By integrating institutional-grade assets with decentralized finance (DeFi) tools, Plume makes traditionally inaccessible assets — like private credit, ETFs, commodities — permissionless, composable, and a click away. Our mission is simple: bridge traditional finance and crypto through demand-driven adoption, without sacrificing transparency, openness, or user experience.

Users deposit stablecoins into vaults, which allocate capital into professionally managed investments — such as bonds, credit products, and other regulated assets — generating yield that is returned to users in the form of vault tokens. Each vault is backed by assets held with licensed custodians and monitored using comprehensive compliance controls including KYC/AML screening and regulatory-aligned permission frameworks. Nest incorporates a programmatic compliance layer to support secure participation while retaining open access where permitted. All smart contracts undergo rigorous security audits, and integrated risk controls ensure asset quality and yield sustainability. Vault tokens are composable across DeFi, enabling users to borrow, trade, or use them within other decentralized applications. By deploying Nest on Canton Network, the platform benefits from Canton’s synchronized ledger technology and privacy-preserving governance, bringing compliant, high-performance tokenized asset exposure to regulated financial institutions, fintechs, and global users in eligible jurisdictions.

Eligible retail users outside of restricted jurisdictions can participate by depositing stablecoins into vaults to earn regulated real-world yield. Institutional users — including asset managers, funds, brokers, and custodians — interact with Nest to originate, support, and distribute tokenized asset products to their clients. Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.
Users will be charged for each Github action they perform. They will also be charged to query the MCP server and they will be charged on a per use basis for any cloud and AI infra which they purchase through the app. This model means that a single developer, using multiple DAML Autopilot features, could create hundreds of transaction per day on the Canton network. Each of these user transactions will contribute to applications rewards.

Canton Coin

For a developer working full time on a project in the Canton ecosystem, it is reasonable to expect between 25 and 150 transactions per day, depending on how heavily they use the toolset.

We expect any developer using these tools for a full time project to generate multiple transactions per round/epoch.

Use Case 1: Automated Yield Compounding SciFeCap is a pure software provider - we never take custody of user assets. Users maintain control of their own wallets at all times. Our DAML smart contracts provide automation that users authorize to execute on their behalf. For yield compounding, users grant our contracts permission to claim rewards from yield-bearing tokens (like USDXLR) and automatically reinvest them. We use Activity Markers - each compounding operation (reward claim + reinvestment) generates Canton Network fees that create Activity Markers. Our smart contracts apply FeaturedAppActivityMarker to these genuine economic activities. Use Case 2: Recursive Position Management For leveraged yield strategies, our infrastructure helps users create and maintain recursive positions (e.g., deposit → borrow → deposit loop on lending protocols). Users authorize our contracts to execute position adjustments and rebalancing. These operations generate multiple transactions during optimization cycles, each creating network fees and Activity Markers. Assets remain in user-controlled wallets throughout. Use Case 3: Portfolio Rebalancing Users can set target allocations across different yield

Bitsafe Vaults is a Canton Network yield vault application based on the ERC-4626 and 7540 standards. It lets users deposit CBTC and other CIP-56 tokens into privacy-preserving, audited vaults that generate yield from on-chain and off-chain strategies. The vault automatically tracks deposits, withdrawals, and rewards using Canton smart contracts, and redistributes Canton Coin rewards back to users as additional yield.
Describe the expected users of your application::
The main users are institutional and qualified DeFi participants who hold CBTC or other Canton assets and want secure yield exposure. Validators, custody providers, and wallet or app developers who host or integrate vault services will also use the system.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.:
Each vault is a DAML contract that manages user deposits and vault shares. Depositing mints new shares and transfers CBTC into a decentralized party that holds assets. Withdrawing burns shares and returns CBTC to the user. Yield and rewards are settled through additional contracts that handle accounting and distribution. All data is private to the involved parties.
Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.:
Rewards are earned when users deposit, withdraw, or when the vault distributes yield. Each of these actions fires a featured activity marker recognized by the network’s reward system.
Does this activity use Canton Coin or Activity Markers to generate rewards?:
The vault uses activity markers to generate Canton Coin rewards instead of CC transfer fees. Under CIP-0078, all CC fees on transfers were removed, so the vault’s activity directly produces reward weight in the Activity Market. The vault can recycle those rewards as additional yield for depositors.
On a per user basis, what is your expected daily number of transactions:

topic.title: Feedback on Featured App Onboarding Process

messages.body:
Hi all,
I’d like to raise some
constructive feedback
regarding the current
Featured App onboarding process
.
A number of organizations — ours
included — have been waiting a significant amount of time to present their Featured Apps to the committee. While we all understand that a careful review is important for ecosystem
 quality and compliance, the
current workflow has created repeated delays and uncertainty
for participants who are actively trying to contribute value to the Canton network.
Specifically:
Teams have prepared to pitch, only for sessions to run out of time.
We were asked to provide
additional information
for asynchronous review/voting — a process that was discontinued shortly after being requested.
As a result, applications now return week after week without clarity on scheduling or progress.
The
weekly one-hour call is consistently stretched
between compliance topics and app evaluations, leaving limited capacity for progress.
This isn’t about blame
— the committee members
 clearly have a heavy load and are doing what they can — but the current mechanism makes it difficult for builders to plan and move forward.
To support both diligence and velocity for the ecosystem, it might be helpful to consider:
A dedicated monthly or bi-weekly Featured App review session
A consistent
asynchronous review
and voting path
A published queue with expected review dates and status
Defined submission criteria so teams can prepare once, not repeatedly
Pre-screen the top ~10 applications
in the queue based on their original submission and pitch deck, and allow them to
receive
 provisional Featured App status
without a live committee pitch. After a 2–3 week period, the committee can review the app’s progress and adherence to the guidelines, and
revoke status if requirements are not met
. This would reward active builders,
 reduce meeting bottlenecks, and allow the committee to focus live time on exceptions and edge cases.
Many of us are
deeply invested
in building real services and infrastructure for the Canton ecosystem. Greater transparency,
 predictability, and throughput in the Featured App pipeline would benefit everyone — including compliance, governance, and the builders working to grow the network.
Thanks for considering this — looking forward to collaborating on ways to streamline the process while maintaining the high standards expected for Featured Apps.
--
With kind regards,
Jan Willem Veldhuis
AKASEC.com

As a builder who has already joined 2 meetings without getting a chance to present, I completely share Jan’s sentiment. Like many teams here, we’re deeply committed to building on Canton and contributing to real network growth, but it’s become increasingly difficult to execute our roadmap when there is no visibility into when our FA proposal will be reviewed.
I strongly support the idea of pre-screening the top ~10 applications (or at least those that have already attended multiple sessions) and granting them provisional FA status while the committee completes its deeper review. This could strike a balance between diligence and momentum.
Looking forward to continuing the discussion and helping the process evolve constructively.
With best regards,
Gregor Arn
HandlPay

Hello Moon processes a meaningful percent of all blockchain transactions, delivering both real-time and historical data. That can be as high as a trillion USD processed in a single day (and growing). Our management team brings decades of experience in software, blockchain, investment banking, private equity, and institutional investing. Our engineering team features top talent from Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Netflix, and more.

Built on Canton’s privacy-preserving and interoperable infrastructure, ACME Lend combines the efficiency of DeFi with the compliance and security required for regulated participants. New Featured App Submission Details
Name of Applying Institution:
FDF Technologies
Summary of Company and Background:
FDF Technologies is a product-driven company that develops and operates its own proprietary
applications within the Canton ecosystem. Leveraging our deep expertise in blockchain and
staking infrastructure, we build secure, high-performing products designed to close critical
market gaps and drive network adoption. We own our product suite, allowing us to align our
long-term success directly with the growth of the Canton network.
Name of the application::
Tokino
URL of the applying institution::
Product Website:
To be provided upon Devnet deployment.
Emails for Responsible Persons::
daniel@...
; lucas@...
Party ID for the Featured Application::
Tokino-validator-1::1220c4e630e0281e7e1487863dd56078106433c35e4bc013f7d3fc8e22464b
c78e28
URL for the public code repository (if available)::
To be provided upon Devnet launch.
Link to Brand Materials (optional):
Demo Video 16:9 ratio (optional):
Provide a summary of what your application will do::
Tokino is envisioned as a foundational building block for DeFi on the Canton Network. Its
mission is to provide a core utility for token holders through an accessible token locking
mechanism. By locking their Canton Coin (CC), participants are incentivized to hold their assets,
which contributes to the network's long-term health and lays the groundwork for them to use
their CC in a future liquid token state. The application's primary function is to increase overall
user loyalty and engagement with the network, which in turn promotes token price stability and
reduces sell pressure. This initial token-locking feature is designed as a foundational step. In the
future, we plan to build on this by issuing a liquid receipt token (tCC) to users, which will serve
as a key building block for a wide range of DeFi activities. For example, the tCC token could be
used as collateral in lending protocols, as a liquidity source on decentralized exchanges, or
deposited into sophisticated yield-generating vaults. The influence of protocols like Lido, ether.fi,
and Ethena on the Ethereum DeFi ecosystem demonstrates the power of this model; their liquid
tokens became fundamental, composable assets that catalyzed innovation in lending, trading,
and yield strategies.
Describe the expected users of your application::
The expected users are all on-ledger parties using the Canton network through a validator. The
application is designed for any party wishing to make their assets productive without needing
deep technical knowledge or running their own infrastructure. The application targets the entire
user base of network participants, aiming to become a foundational earning and liquidity tool for
any party on the network holding CC.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.:
The Tokino application will consist of Daml smart contracts implemented on the Canton Net

Provide a summary of what your application will do::
Register Labs – Compliance Platform A blockchain-enabled compliance platform for managing and verifying supplier documentation. Companies can request vendors to upload due-diligence files (such as ISO, insurance, and safety certificates) into secure AWS storage. Each upload or update automatically generates a verifiable, tamper-proof record on the Canton network, ensuring an auditable history without exposing sensitive data. Built for privacy, efficiency, and trust in enterprise supply chains.
Describe the expected users of your application::
The application is designed for companies and institutions that need to manage supplier due diligence and compliance documentation in a secure, auditable way. Typical users include compliance teams, procurement departments, and risk managers within financial institutions, corporates, and service providers. Initial adoption is expected from validators and ecosystem participants on the Canton Network who wish to centralize and automate their vendor compliance workflows. We’ve already had some initial conversations with validators on the Canton network, and a handful have expressed interest in using the app to centralise and manage their vendor due diligence documents. We can provide the names separately if needed.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.:
The ledger interactions stated in the next question will not utilise Canton’s Activity Markets for app rewards. Instead, the platform will integrate with Cantara for scripture-based, on-chain billing and payment processing.
Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.:
When a supplier uploads a document, the file is hashed using SHA-256, and a SupplierCertificate Daml contract is created on Canton. That contract anchors the hash and key metadata on the ledger, providing an immutable record without storing the underlying file. If a supplier later renews or updates a document, the previous contract is archived and replaced with a new one via Daml ‘choices,’ preserving a complete, verifiable audit history. Canton can also be queried to verify a document’s authenticity by matching the stored hash..
Does this activity use Canton Coin or Activity Markers to generate rewards?:
No, we plan to use Cantara.
On a per user basis, what is your expected daily number of transactions:
Each active user will generate approximately 144 Cantara billing transaction per day (1 per 144-block cycle a day) for subscription processing. Beyond that, we expect only occasional ledger int

Hi Amanda (and Committee),
Thanks for today’s session. I appreciate the packed agenda, thank you for agreeing to review our
 information asynchronously.
Here’s what I planned to present:
Recorded presentation (3 mins):
https://www.loom.com/share/db7199ad2c22427e81553eb40b81da78
Loom demo (2.3 mins):
https://www.loom.com/share/d79545551ff04b7fa7af2e8720f0a095
Slide deck (PDF): attached
I’m happy to answer questions or join a short call. Key points are below.
Kind regards,
Oliver
Quick summary
Register Labs’ Supplier Compliance Platform helps companies collect, manage, and prove supplier
 compliance (ISO/SOC, insurance, safety). Files stay private in the customer’s encrypted cloud storage; the Canton network acts as a tamper-evident notary for the events around those files.
How we interact with Canton (no Activity Markets)
Each important action (upload, renewal, revoke, co-sign) is hashed (SHA-256) and written as a Daml contract on Canton.
The ledger stores only the hash + minimal metadata; no raw documents touch the chain (files remain in the customer’s encrypted AWS S3).
For high-value submissions, buyer and supplier co-sign the same on-chain receipt to remove “sent/received/version” disputes.
Customers can grant named observers (e.g., auditors/insurers) permission to append short, signed attestations to the on-chain record.
Light rate-limits prevent spammy or non-bona-fide edits.
We do not use Activity Markets.
Billing & rewards
Subscription billing will be processed on-chain via the DA Billing Utility (or Cantara, as applicable) for customers who prefer that route; non-validator customers can pay via fiat/Stripe.
The only reward-generating activity is the on-chain subscription billing event. Document events do not generate rewards.
Pricing starts from $300/month.
Go-live readiness
Initial adopters are ready to start: a mix of organisations that run a Canton validator and others that do not.

Name of Applying Institution:

a831fc43-f685-4e1f-9db2-629034bc514a::1220921c9c5579f2648609d8b007793ffbe31efb311c79c53cbc68571827c1c5ebee
URL for the public code repository (if available)::
Link to Brand Materials (optional):
Demo Video 16:9 ratio (optional):
Provide a summary of what your application will do::
Our platform, built on the Canton Network (DAML), modernizes the initial book building for tokenized asset offerings.

The system facilitates Issuer onboarding and asset tokenization, initiating the offering. Investors participate by securing commitments through a controlled Deposit action. Funds are held in secure escrow wallets, represented by an on-ledger, single-investor deposit contract.

Crucially, the platform provides real-time, verifiable visibility into on-chain locked balances for both the Issuer and the Investor, ensuring complete transparency. Control is absolute: the Deposit action requires multi-party signatures—specifically from the Custodian and the Investor. This prevents unilateral fund movement, establishing the necessary institutional rigor before proceeding to DvP settlement.
Describe the expected users of your application::
Investors, Issuers, Underwriters & Custodian
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.:
Interaction with the Canton ledger is executed primarily by the Custodian, who is responsible for initiating all actions via the Ledger API. The core data unit on the ledger is the DepositContract, a Contract Instance that serves as the unique, on-ledger representation of a deposit address for a single Investor. The Investor, Custodian, and Issuer are all represented as a Party, an identified entity on Canton, and can each run on separate Participant Nodes.

Operations on this contract are executed through specific Choices—Deposit, DvP Settlement, and Withdrawal. Each action requires specific parties to act as Signatories, providing the requisite signature to authorize the change in contract state. The Custodian is the Controller for all choices, but the requirement for a second signature (e.g., Investor for Deposit, Issuer for DvP) enforces multi-party control.

Finally, the application uses Canton’s Transaction View m

Thetanuts Finance specializes in building decentralized options products and infrastructure. We've launched an open-source options infrastructure layer that protocols, builders, and users leverage to create option products, strategies, and structured products. We currently operate live on over 7 chains, running innovative Option Vault strategies that generate sustainable, organic yield through option premiums. To date, we've sold nearly $2 billion in notional value through these strategies. Our expertise is in creating DeFi Option Vaults (DOVs) that allow users and counterparties to earn consistent returns. We've built the infrastructure, proven it at scale, and established long-term relationships with institutional market makers who provide liquidity across our platforms.

Describe the expected users of your application::

Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.:
The module creates subscription and billing transactions on-chain per epoch. It reads ledger state for analytics (e.g., validator rewards, wallet balances, bridge flows) and records usage metrics for reward calculation.

topic.title: New Featured App Request: PeerTrust Ltd

messages.body:
New Featured App Submission Details
Name of Applying Institution:
PeerTrust Ltd
Name of the application::
PeerTrust
URL of the applying institution::
www.peertrust.io
Emails for Responsible Persons::
josh@...
Party ID for the Featured Application::
peertrust-validator-1::1220a35b88ad9227d145ca396ac92136ee6c694b1e20c4f9ac5d1de2d39a1c3864a6
URL for the public code repository (if available)::
N/A
Provide a summary of what your application will do::
PeerTrust is an HR credential platform that lets organisations collect, track, and prove staff work-readiness: right-to-work, background checks, licences, qualifications, immunisations, and safety training. Files stay private in encrypted storage, while a tamper-evident proof is anchored to the Canton network, giving HR a live renewal diary, auditors an independent evidence trail, and employees a simple way to stay compliant, without exposing sensitive data.
Describe the expected users of your application::
Primary users: HR teams, compliance officers, and operations leads responsible for employee/contractor documentation in regulated or safety-critical sectors (financial services, healthcare, construction, logistics, education). Secondary stakeholders include auditors (read-only verification) and employees/contractors (self-service submissions). Early adopters: Institutions and validators in the Canton ecosystem that need verifiable workforce records for audits and internal controls. We’ve already held initial discussions with several validators who see strong value in a privacy-preserving credential layer that anchors proofs on Canton while keeping documents off-chain.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.:
Issue/Upload: When HR or an employee submits a credential, the system computes a SHA-256 hash of the file and writes a time-stamped attestation to Canton via an EmployeeCredential contract. The attestation records the fingerprint, credential class, validity dates, lifecycle status, and the relevant parties. No file data is put on-chain; the document remains in encrypted storage.

Renew/Replace: Updates create a new versioned attestation and archive the prior one, producing a complete, queryable lineage of that credential over time.

Revoke: A revoke action updates status on-chain and closes the credential’s active lineage while keeping prior versions auditable.

Verify: Later checks recompute the file’s fingerprint and compare it with the on-chain attestation to confirm integrity and timing, proving what was submitted and when without exposing the underlying document.

Reads vs. writes: Routine dashboard views and audits use read-only lookups; writes occur only on lifecycle events (issue, renew/replace, revoke).
Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.:
Only subscription payments (processed via the DA Utility or Cantara) will be reward-eligible.
Does this activity use Canton Coin or Activity Markers to generate rewards?:
No Activity Mark

Hi Amanda,
Thank you for the update. I’m happy to proceed with the async vote.
Please find some additional materials attached to be included with my original application for PeerTrust. I have provided a video of the presentation I planned to deliver and an executive summary.
Do let me know if you need anything else from me, if you have any issues accessing either of the attached files, or if you have any questions.
Kind regards,
Josh

URL for the public code repository (if available)::
Provide a summary of what your application will do::
Its support of Canton network on the Cypherock hardware wallet, self custodian that relies on external party IDs. The users can send/receive Canton coins and other tokens that adhere to the token standard. This also covers support on the cySync app (both desktop and mobile) and a web wallet through which the users can manage both a software and a hardware wallet.
Describe the expected users of your application::
Our audience is existing Cypherock wallet users & end users who want to get exposure to the canton network in a way that allows them full custody of their assets on the safest hardware wallet.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.:
It's a native Canton network support, so every transfer happens on-ledger. With our planned integrations, there will be even more indirect ledger usage, through 3rd party apps.
Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.:
Tranfers. Onboarding. Swaps.
Does this activity use Canton Coin or Activity Markers to generate rewards?:
Launching with activity markers and will eventually have canton coin transfers for some premium features.
On a per user basis, what is your expected daily number of transactions:
Hard to estimate right now but should be atleast 1-10 once we integrate
https://canton.exchange
Under what conditions may a user generate multiple transactions per round/epoch?:
Multiple p2p transfers, swaps or payroll execution
How do you expect your transactions to scale as your customer base scales?  E.g. Linearly, Super Linear, Sub Linear.:
Linear
What is your anticipated launch date on MainNet?:
November 1st 2025
Who will be your first customers and what is the expected go-live dates?:
Cypherock wallet existing users, MPCH, Tenkai. Probably same as the launch date.
How would not having FA status change your operating plans?:
It wouldn't but given that we want to incentivize usage and growth, we'd rather have it.
Does your application have any controls to prevent non-bona fide transactions?:
Only users who have purchased a Cypherock X1 will be able to interact with the application to begin with.
Additional Notes for the Committee’s consideration::
Demo -
https://youtu.be/XK9AkgXwZO8
Note:
Next step is to have them demo the App at the next meeting. Any questions or concerns before proceeding?

Hi Amanda and the rest of the committee,
Thank you for agreeing to vote for our app asynchronously. We studied and talked to other wallet builders and decided to go with Canton coin transfers for app rewards generation instead of activity markers. There was initially confusion around how activity markers worked, which got resolved. Secondly, as Eric suggested, we have synced with Wayne to cater to the network scaling requirements for a wallet.
Here’s what we had presented:
Cypherock hardware wallet demo -
https://youtu.be/LjF0cBfv8CY
Slides -
https://gamma.app/docs/Cypherock-Hardware-Wallet-on-Canton-Network-8e8g95nd91b4q9m?mode=doc
I’m happy to answer questions or join a short call if required. Key points that got updated are below.
1. Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from?
Canton coin transfers
2. Does this activity use Canton Coin or Activity Markers to generate rewards?
Canton coin transfers
Regards,
Rohan

New Featured App Submission Details
Name of Applying Institution:

Summary of Company and Background:
Software development company focused on Defi blockchain applications. Have launched DEX, prediction markets and multichain apps.

Provide a summary of what your application will do::
Unhedged is a decentralized prediction market platform built on the Canton Network. It introduces a time-weighted rewards mechanism that incentivizes early participation and accurate forecasting in binary and multiple-choice markets. By using smart contract-based escrow and eliminating pooled bets, Unhedged ensures fairer payouts and minimizes platform risk. The system is fully on-chain, privacy-preserving, and leverages Canton Coin (CC) as its native currency along with other tokens on the Canton Network.
Describe the expected users of your application::
Our expected users include: Crypto-native retail users interested in predictive finance and decentralized forecasting Data-savvy individuals who want to monetize their insights on political, financial, or sports-related events Communities organizing permissionless prediction events Institutions or DAOs looking to create private or branded markets for internal forecasting or public engagement
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.:
Unhedged uses Canton Coin (CC) as the primary medium of exchange for placing bets, locking funds in per-market escrow contracts built with Daml smart contracts. Each user action (e.g., market creation, bet placement, claims) is an on-ledger transaction, fully trackable and auditable.

For Activity Markets, Unhedged can integrate markers for:

- Early-bird bets
- Active Market Participation (top bettors, biggest winners, biggest pnl etc.)
- Market Curation and moderation

These markers would help measure genuine engagement and prediction activity, qualifying the app for ongoing Activity Marker-based application rewards.


Unhedged interacts directly with the Canton ledger through a suite of over 35 Daml contracts. Key interactions include:

- Market creation (admin)
- Token escrow and settlement per market
- Payouts processed automatically after market resolution
- Full audit trails for every bet and claim
- Emergency controls (e.g., market cancellation)

All user and admin actions generate ledger entries, with no off-chain settlement for critical functions.
Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.:
We expect to earn rewards from:

- Hi

New Featured App Submission Details
Name of Applying Institution:

URL for the public code repository (if available)::
Provide a summary of what your application will do::
Excellar issues regulated yield-bearing tokens (xlTokens) on Canton denominated in BTC, ETH, and USD etc. We're the first Bermuda-licensed platform bringing institutional-grade Bitcoin and dollar yields to Canton Network, enabling enterprises, institutions, PSPs, and DeFi protocols to earn compliant returns while maintaining regulatory compliance. Future updates will include the launch of tools and protocols for analytics, lending, borrowing, and trading using Excellar-issued tokens and other assets available on the Canton Network.
Describe the expected users of your application::
Payment Service Providers generating yield on float (first client: ScopeX in Europe-India corridor), institutions and corporate treasuries seeking compliant yield on Bitcoin holdings, reinsurance captives and family offices requiring yield-bearing collateral, Canton DeFi applications using xlTokens for collateral, liquidity, and structured products, and institutional liquidity providers and market makers.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.:
Excellar deploys Daml smart contracts managing token lifecycle: minting when assets are deposited with institutional custodians, automatic yield accrual/NAV updates, privacy-preserving transfers between participants, and burning upon redemption. Tokens interoperate atomically with other Canton applications through the Global Synchronizer.
Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.:
Minting (10%): Creating tokens when users deposit assets. Burning (10%): Redeeming tokens back to underlying assets. Transfers (80%): P2P payments, DeFi interactions, cross-border settlements, yield distributions, and collateral movements.
Does this activity use Canton Coin or Activity Markers to generate rewards?:
Activity Markers. Our transactions generate network fees that get burned, creating Activity Markers proportional to the fee value. These accumulate in 10-minute rounds, and our Featured App rewards are allocated based on our Activity Marker share relative to other apps in each round.
On a per user basis, what is your expected daily number of transactions:
PSPs: 100-300 transactions/day (e.g., ScopeX cross-border payments). DeFi Protocols: 10-50 transactions/day (collateral, liquidity, arbitrage). Treasuries: 2-5 transactions/day (rebalancing, yield collection). Reinsurance/Captives: 1-5

Provide a summary of what your application will do::
CantonGo is a featured analytics and infrastructure platform on the Canton Network, offering a number of unique features like metahub name service, app directory, ecosystem hub, and powerful data insights, designed to enhance network transparency, validator performance, and ecosystem growth.

It operates in two tiers:

Public Layer: a high-performance explorer, analytics hub, and data API that exposes validator activity, transactions, and ecosystem insights to all network participants.

Enterprise Layer: a validator-integrated analytics suite offering real-time data feeds, custom dashboards, and private APIs for applications, validators, and service providers. 
As a featured app, CantonGo directly contributes to network utility by processing, categorizing, and indexing activity markers, and by providing mission-critical data infrastructure for all ecosystem participants.
Describe the expected users of your application::
Users: Validators, Application Teams, Institutional Users, Data Analysts, Developers, and Ecosystem Integrators.
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.:
CantonGo is natively integrated with validator infrastructure, indexing on-chain activity, validator telemetry, transaction data, ledger events, and contract interactions. It structures this data into real-time analytics, dashboards, and network health reports — available via API and private data feeds for enterprise customers.
Describe the activities that your application will earn application rewards from.:
The platform will utilize Canton Coin (CC) for various transactions, including subscription payments from onboarded validators, enterprise API calls, real-time analytics requests, and validator data integrations.
Each activity (data request, analytics query, or validator interaction) produces Activity Markers, generating measurable and genuine application rewards that scale with network adoption.
Does this activity use Canton Coin or Activity Markers to generate rewards?:
CantonGo will use Canton Coin as its primary payment method, facilitating transactions and generating rewards through the Activity Marker system for validated data and analytics events.
On a per user basis, what is your expected daily number of transactions:
Expected daily transactions per user: Up to 144
Under what conditions may a user generate multiple transactions per round/epoch?:
We do not expect such conditions.
How do you expect your transactions to scale as your customer base scales?  E.g. Linearly, Super Linea

Hi,
I have a couple of items.
A) I see "Canton" is being used as part of the app name. We recently were told by the DA team that all FAs are being asked to stop using the Canton trademark in their app name. I think we should be addressing this with FA applicants too.
B) The expected number of transactions per user does not correspond with the description of where featured app markers would be used. 144 daily txs per user would only cover subscription billings.
If the app were to produce additional markers every time that a user requested data via API, or data was published, or any sort of high-throughput activity like that, then:
- The number of transactions per user would be many multiples higher than 144.
- There's a bigger question as to whether a basic user action, such as making an API call to a private server, is a good use of the CC allocation for FA incentives.
Thanks
--
Juan Costa
COO

Hello!
As a Reply to the thread
https://lists.sync.global/g/tokenomics/message/739
Question:
Does this activity use Canton Coin or Activity Markers to generate rewards?
Answer:
As agreed on the call, we are providing the details on the Canton Coin payments and ActivityMarkers generation for our app, CantonGo:
CantonGo Public explorer: it is a public good, no payments, no ActivityMarkers
,
CantonGo Private app that will be installed on the validator infrastructure and provide the information about the validator's private data (transactions, updates, active contracts, statistics): Canton Coin is used for subscription payments, ActivityMarkers are generated automatically as part of the subscription payment transaction.
,
CantonGo API access: Users will be buying API Access Tokens on-chain using Canon Coin as a means of payment. Each API Access Token will be a contract on the Canton ledger, with an expiry date. Upon the exchange of the Canton Coin for an API Access Token, the ActivityMarker will be generated, confirming the transfer of the API Access Token to the user on-chain.
,
--------------------
Viachaslau H.
Co-founder & Communications Lead
Staketab
Github
Website

messages.body:
New Featured App Submission Details
Name of Applying Institution:
DTEAM
Summary of Company and Background:
DTEAM is a professional validator and infrastructure provider operating across 20+ mainnets and 30+ testnets. We operate validator nodes and provide dedicated full/archive node deployments with fast setup and 99.9% uptime. We develop and maintain open-source tools and infrastructure that strengthen PoS ecosystems and make network participation more efficient.
Name of the application:

Disclaimer 
You may not use the Canton name, logo, or any other Canton trademark in, or as part of, your product or service name.

BRAND GUIDLINES:
Transparent Scan
URL of the applying institution::
https://dteam.tech/
Product Website:
https://transparentscan.xyz/
Emails for Responsible Persons::
eugene@..., ivan@...
Party ID for the Featured Application::
N/A
URL for the public code repository (if available)::
N/A
Link to Brand Materials (optional):
https://github.com/DTEAMTECH/identity
Demo Video 16:9 ratio (optional):
Provide a summary of what your application will do::
v1 Transparent Scan is a live app built for validators and users within the Canton ecosystem. It forms a structured marketplace where validators can offer RPС access, and users can connect to these endpoints through a paid subscription. This model allows validators to monetize their infrastructure while providing developers and ecosystem participants with stable RPC access to the network.

The platform tracks subscribed validator performance - uptime, latency, and reliability - and distributes rewards based on these metrics. High-performing validators gain higher visibility and a greater share of subscription revenue, creating a transparent and self-sustaining incentive system that improves overall network quality.

v2 Transparent Scan (next development stage) will evolve Transparent Scan into a full-featured network explorer with its own public APIs.

This version will introduce analytics dashboards, performance insights, transaction monitoring, and real-time telemetry tools. Ecosystem developers will be able to integrate these APIs directly into their products, expanding transparency across the Canton network and creating new points of interaction between validators, users, and applications.
Describe the expected users of your application::
Developers, applications, validators
Describe how your application will interact with the ledger.:
For now, here is our approximate flow: Our team manually reviews and merges a validator’s GitHub PR (access to the repository will be available soon), our backend service receives the merge webhook and submits a command to the JSON Ledger API to create the validator’s listing and a subscription contract. API purchases and endpoint allowances are also modeled as Daml contracts and created through the JSON Ledger API. Uptime is measured off-ledger via periodic /livez checks and exposed through our public API, while revenue distribution remains on-ledger.
Desc

Canton Finance is building the first Asia-based, compliant on-chain yield and asset issuance platform on the Canton Network.
Our platform enables users to deposit and over-collateralize digital assets — such as $USDT, $USDC, and $BTC — to mint $USDA (Asian USD), a network-native stable asset designed to deliver transparent and regulated yield across the APAC region. We are positioned to become the leading B2C DeFi gateway on Canton Network in Asia.
The Vault (MVP) is the first product, allowing users to lock $USDT / $USDC to mint $USDA directly on Canton Network. Rewards generated from Canton Finance’s validator operations are distributed to depositors, creating real on-chain yield and marking the first phase of our consumer go-to-market strategy.
Next, the Canton Finance team will launch an Aave-style Liquidity Market, where users can supply liquidity, borrow assets, and earn yield — enhanced by the privacy, security, and performance of the Canton Network.
In parallel, Canton Finance is pursuing full regulatory compliance, beginning with obtaining a stablecoin issuer license in Hong Kong. The licensed version, $USDA, will act as a bridge between traditional finance and decentralized finance, channeling secure, institution-grade yield from regulated financial markets to digital assets like $BTC.

Stage One of Canton Finance focuses on promoting and popularizing Canton Network DeFi among the large retail markets in Chinese-speaking regions, Korea, and Southeast Asia — communities that are eager to engage with and grow alongside emerging on-chain projects. messages.body:

Name of Applying Institution:
Ninteen26 Ltd
Summary of Company and Background:
PropNotary is a blockchain-enabled verification platform designed to bring integrity and traceability to professional document verification. It allows authorised professionals (lawyers, surveyors, accountants, consultants) to attest and anchor documents on the Canton blockchain, generating a permanent proof of authenticity without exposing sensitive data.  Each verification event records a cryptographic hash and timestamp on-chain, creating a tamper-proof audit trail that can be independently verified at any time.  Built for privacy, accountability, and interoperability, PropNotary provides a foundation for a multi-sector “trust directory” of verified professionals and their authenticated documents

United App connects to the Canton Network as an AI-powered information and analytics layer. It aggregates public data, ecosystem metrics, and project updates while enabling users to follow and engage with Canton-based applications. Premium features will be paid in Canton Coin.
